title: Day 1 Vocabulary — 600+ Essential English Words for Basics of English
description: Comprehensive 1000-word vocabulary bank covering everyday English, business, office communication, career, academic, advanced adjectives/verbs, phrasal expressions, and technology & digital — all with IPA, Hindi meanings, 6-context sentences, synonyms, antonyms, and usage notes.

words.simpleMeaning: to do a job or task

sentences.daily: I work from 9 AM to 6 PM every day.
sentences.office: She works in the finance department.
sentences.interview: I have worked in IT for three years.
sentences.business: Our team works efficiently to meet deadlines.
sentences.formal: The committee works to improve public health.
sentences.informal: He works like a machine — never stops!

words.usageNote: 'Work' as noun = the job itself. 'Work' as verb = doing the job. Never say 'I do work' when you mean 'I work'.

words.simpleMeaning: to gain knowledge or skill

sentences.daily: I learn something new every day.
sentences.office: She learns new software quickly.
sentences.interview: I learn from my mistakes and improve.
sentences.business: Our employees learn through regular training.
sentences.formal: Students learn best in interactive environments.
sentences.informal: He never learns from his mistakes!

words.usageNote: Both 'learned' and 'learnt' are correct. 'Learned' = American, 'learnt' = British.

words.simpleMeaning: to say words, to talk

sentences.daily: I speak Hindi and English fluently.
sentences.office: Please speak clearly in the meeting.
sentences.interview: I speak confidently about my achievements.
sentences.business: Our CEO speaks at international conferences.
sentences.formal: The ambassador speaks on behalf of the nation.
sentences.informal: Just speak up! Don't be shy.

words.usageNote: Use 'speak' for languages and formal situations. Use 'talk' for casual conversations.

words.simpleMeaning: to know the meaning of something

sentences.daily: Do you understand what I am saying?
sentences.office: I understand the project requirements completely.
sentences.interview: I understand complex technical concepts easily.
sentences.business: We need to understand customer needs first.
sentences.formal: The court understands the gravity of the situation.
sentences.informal: I just don't understand why he did that!

words.usageNote: State verb — do NOT use in continuous form. Say 'I understand' NOT 'I am understanding'.

words.simpleMeaning: to assist someone or make something easier

sentences.daily: Can you help me carry these bags?
sentences.office: She helps new employees settle in.
sentences.interview: I always help my team members when needed.
sentences.business: Our product helps businesses save time.
sentences.formal: The government helps citizens through welfare schemes.
sentences.informal: Don't worry, I'll help you out!

words.usageNote: 'Help' can be followed by bare infinitive: 'Help me do' OR 'Help me to do' — both correct.

words.simpleMeaning: to look at written words and understand them

sentences.daily: I read the newspaper every morning.
sentences.office: She reads all client emails carefully.
sentences.interview: I read industry reports to stay updated.
sentences.business: We read customer feedback to improve.
sentences.formal: The law requires you to read the contract.
sentences.informal: I read novels before sleeping every night.

words.usageNote: Past tense of 'read' is also spelled 'read' but pronounced /rɛd/ (like 'red').

words.simpleMeaning: to make letters, words, or sentences on paper or screen

sentences.daily: I write in my diary every night.
sentences.office: She writes all project reports.
sentences.interview: I write professional emails and proposals.
sentences.business: Our team writes detailed documentation.
sentences.formal: The author writes about social issues.
sentences.informal: He writes really cool song lyrics!

words.usageNote: Homophones: write/right/rite. 'Write' is the verb. 'Right' = correct/direction.

words.simpleMeaning: to put food in your mouth and swallow it

sentences.daily: I eat breakfast at 8 AM every day.
sentences.office: We eat lunch together in the cafeteria.
sentences.interview: I eat healthy to maintain high energy.
sentences.business: Let's eat together — it builds team culture.
sentences.formal: One should eat nutritious food daily.
sentences.informal: Let's eat pizza tonight — I'm starving!

words.usageNote: 'Eat' is irregular: eat → ate → eaten. Don't say 'I eated' — it's 'I ate'.

words.simpleMeaning: to take liquid into your mouth and swallow

sentences.daily: I drink 8 glasses of water daily.
sentences.office: She drinks coffee before every meeting.
sentences.interview: I don't drink alcohol — I stay focused.
sentences.business: Our office provides free drinks for employees.
sentences.formal: One should drink clean water to stay healthy.
sentences.informal: He drinks chai like it's going out of style!

words.usageNote: 'Drink' is irregular: drink → drank → drunk. 'Drunk' as adjective = intoxicated.

words.simpleMeaning: to move from one place to another

sentences.daily: I go to the gym every morning.
sentences.office: She goes to the office by metro.
sentences.interview: I go the extra mile for every project.
sentences.business: Our sales team goes to client sites weekly.
sentences.formal: Citizens go to vote during elections.
sentences.informal: Where do you go on weekends?

words.usageNote: Highly irregular: go → went → gone. 'Goes' for he/she/it. 'Going to' shows future plans.

words.simpleMeaning: to move toward the speaker or a place

sentences.daily: Can you come to my house today?
sentences.office: Please come to the office by 9 AM.
sentences.interview: I come prepared for every interview.
sentences.business: The CEO comes to team meetings personally.
sentences.formal: Citizens come together during national festivals.
sentences.informal: Come on, let's go already!

words.usageNote: 'Come' is irregular: come → came → come. 'Come' for invitation; 'go' from speaker's side.

words.simpleMeaning: to notice something with your eyes

sentences.daily: I see the mountains from my window.
sentences.office: I see potential in this project.
sentences.interview: I see myself growing in this company.
sentences.business: We see a 30% growth opportunity.
sentences.formal: The doctor sees patients in the morning.
sentences.informal: Did you see that movie last night?

words.usageNote: State verb — avoid continuous: 'I see' NOT 'I am seeing'. 'See' = notice. 'Look' = direct attention.

words.simpleMeaning: to direct your eyes at something

sentences.daily: Look at this beautiful sunset!
sentences.office: Please look at the report I sent.
sentences.interview: Look at my portfolio for past work.
sentences.business: Look at these quarterly results.
sentences.formal: Look carefully before you sign.
sentences.informal: Look, I need to tell you something.

words.usageNote: 'Look at' needs 'at'. 'Look' = intentional. 'See' = automatic. 'Watch' = moving things.

words.simpleMeaning: to pay attention to sounds

sentences.daily: I listen to English podcasts every morning.
sentences.office: Please listen carefully in the meeting.
sentences.interview: I always listen to the interviewer first.
sentences.business: We listen to customer complaints seriously.
sentences.formal: Students should listen attentively in class.
sentences.informal: Just listen — I have something important to say.

words.usageNote: 'Listen to' needs 'to'. 'Listen' = intentional. 'Hear' = automatic. Don't say 'listen something'.

words.simpleMeaning: to speak to someone about something

sentences.daily: I talk to my parents every evening.
sentences.office: Let's talk about the project tomorrow.
sentences.interview: I enjoy talking about my achievements.
sentences.business: We need to talk about the budget.
sentences.formal: The professor talks about social issues.
sentences.informal: We talk for hours without getting bored!

words.usageNote: 'Talk to' = general. 'Talk with' = two-way. 'Talk about' = subject. More casual than 'speak'.

words.simpleMeaning: to use your mind to form ideas or make decisions

sentences.daily: I think before I speak.
sentences.office: I think this plan will work well.
sentences.interview: I think I am a good problem-solver.
sentences.business: I think this merger will benefit both companies.
sentences.formal: One must think carefully before deciding.
sentences.informal: What do you think? Tell me honestly!

words.usageNote: State verb used in continuous for current thought: 'I'm thinking about it' (in progress).

words.simpleMeaning: to have information or be certain about something

sentences.daily: I know the way to the station.
sentences.office: Do you know the company's annual revenue?
sentences.interview: I know how to handle pressure.
sentences.business: We know our target market very well.
sentences.formal: Every citizen should know their rights.
sentences.informal: I know, I know — I should have called!

words.usageNote: State verb — avoid continuous: 'I know' NOT 'I am knowing'. 'Know' = knowledge/fact.

words.simpleMeaning: to hand something to someone

sentences.daily: Give me five minutes, please.
sentences.office: Please give me the project update.
sentences.interview: Can you give an example of your skills?
sentences.business: We give clients a 30-day free trial.
sentences.formal: The government gives subsidies to farmers.
sentences.informal: Give it a try — you never know!

words.usageNote: 'Give' is irregular: give → gave → given. Patterns: 'give someone something' OR 'give something to someone'.

words.simpleMeaning: to get hold of something or move with you

sentences.daily: Take a deep breath and relax.
sentences.office: Please take note of the action items.
sentences.interview: I take initiative in every project.
sentences.business: We take quality seriously in our company.
sentences.formal: The court takes evidence very seriously.
sentences.informal: Take it easy — it's not that hard!

words.usageNote: 'Take' is irregular: take → took → taken. Many phrasal verbs: take off, take over, take up.

words.simpleMeaning: to create or produce something

sentences.daily: I make breakfast every morning.
sentences.office: She makes important decisions daily.
sentences.interview: I make sure every deadline is met.
sentences.business: We make products that change lives.
sentences.formal: The committee makes rules for the public.
sentences.informal: You make me laugh every single day!

words.usageNote: 'Make' is irregular: make → made → made. 'Make' for creation; 'do' for actions/tasks.

words.simpleMeaning: to own, possess, or experience something

sentences.daily: I have two brothers and one sister.
sentences.office: We have a meeting at 3 PM today.
sentences.interview: I have excellent communication skills.
sentences.business: We have 500 employees across 5 cities.
sentences.formal: Every citizen has the right to vote.
sentences.informal: I have the best friends in the world!

words.usageNote: 'Have' as main verb = possess. 'Have' as auxiliary = perfect tense. Never 'I am having a car'.

words.simpleMeaning: to do something with a tool, method, or skill

sentences.daily: I use my phone for studying English.
sentences.office: She uses Excel for all financial reports.
sentences.interview: I use data to make informed decisions.
sentences.business: We use technology to serve customers better.
sentences.formal: Doctors use advanced tools for diagnosis.
sentences.informal: Use your brain, not just your phone!

words.usageNote: 'Use' as noun = /juːs/ (a use). 'Use' as verb = /juːz/ (to use). Different pronunciation!

words.simpleMeaning: to require something or feel it is necessary

sentences.daily: I need to buy some groceries.
sentences.office: We need to finish the report by Friday.
sentences.interview: I need a team player for this role.
sentences.business: Our company needs more marketing budget.
sentences.formal: The country needs strong leadership.
sentences.informal: I need coffee — it's an emergency!

words.usageNote: Both state verb (I need your help) and modal verb (You need not worry). Most common as main verb.

words.simpleMeaning: to desire or wish for something

sentences.daily: I want to learn French next year.
sentences.office: She wants to lead the marketing team.
sentences.interview: I want to grow within this company.
sentences.business: We want to expand to international markets.
sentences.formal: Every student wants quality education.
sentences.informal: I just want to sleep for 12 hours!

words.usageNote: State verb — avoid continuous when expressing desire: 'I want' NOT 'I am wanting'. Exception in polite contexts.

words.simpleMeaning: to make an effort to do something

sentences.daily: I try to exercise every day.
sentences.office: We try to exceed client expectations.
sentences.interview: I always try my best in everything.
sentences.business: We try new strategies every quarter.
sentences.formal: One must try to learn from failure.
sentences.informal: Just try it — what's the worst that can happen?

words.usageNote: 'Try to do' = attempt. 'Try doing' = experiment. Irregular in past: try → tried (y→i+ed).

words.simpleMeaning: to move fast or to operate/manage something

sentences.daily: I run 5 km every morning.
sentences.office: She runs the entire marketing division.
sentences.interview: I have run multiple successful projects.
sentences.business: We run a 24/7 customer support team.
sentences.formal: The government runs public schools.
sentences.informal: Run! We are going to miss the bus!

words.usageNote: Irregular: run → ran → run. 'Run' a business = manage it. 'Run' = physical movement.

words.simpleMeaning: to receive, obtain, or become

sentences.daily: I get up at 6 AM every day.
sentences.office: I got the promotion last month.
sentences.interview: Where can I get the application form?
sentences.business: We get orders from 20 countries.
sentences.formal: Citizens get free healthcare in this country.
sentences.informal: Get ready — the party starts now!

words.usageNote: Most versatile verb in English. 'Get' = receive/obtain/become. Many phrasal verbs: get up, get over, get along.

words.simpleMeaning: to place something in a position

sentences.daily: Put your shoes near the door.
sentences.office: Please put your signature on the form.
sentences.interview: I put my best effort into everything.
sentences.business: Put the figures in the report.
sentences.formal: Please put the documents on my desk.
sentences.informal: Just put it down — you look silly!

words.usageNote: Irregular: put → put → put (no change). 'Put' = placement. 'Put up with' = tolerate.

words.simpleMeaning: to shout someone's name or contact by phone

sentences.daily: I call my mother every evening.
sentences.office: Please call the client before the meeting.
sentences.interview: Call me anytime — I am always available.
sentences.business: Please call our support at 1800-XXX-XXXX.
sentences.formal: The doctor calls the patient's name.
sentences.informal: Call me! We need to catch up soon.

words.usageNote: 'Call' = phone call in modern usage. 'Call someone' = phone them. 'Call someone something' = name them.

words.simpleMeaning: to encounter someone, usually for the first time or by arrangement

sentences.daily: I meet new people at every event.
sentences.office: We meet every Monday for team standup.
sentences.interview: Pleased to meet you, Mr. Sharma.
sentences.business: We meet clients before finalizing contracts.
sentences.formal: The leaders meet to discuss peace.
sentences.informal: So nice to meet you at last!

words.usageNote: Irregular: meet → met → met. 'Meet' = encounter. 'Meat' = food. Same pronunciation, different meaning!

words.simpleMeaning: to get something by paying money for it

sentences.daily: I buy vegetables from the local market.
sentences.office: The company buys office supplies monthly.
sentences.interview: I always buy within my budget.
sentences.business: We buy in bulk to reduce costs.
sentences.formal: The government buys food for public distribution.
sentences.informal: Let me buy you coffee — my treat!

words.usageNote: Irregular: buy → bought → bought. 'By' and 'buy' sound same but different spelling/meaning.

words.simpleMeaning: to exchange something for money

sentences.daily: He sells vegetables near our house.
sentences.office: We sell software to enterprise clients.
sentences.interview: I have sold products worth ₹10 lakh.
sentences.business: We sell our products in 15 countries.
sentences.formal: Farmers sell crops at the market.
sentences.informal: She sells handmade jewelry online!

words.usageNote: Irregular: sell → sold → sold. 'Sell' = verb. 'Sale' = noun. 'Cell' = unrelated (phone/biology).

words.simpleMeaning: to move something so it is no longer closed

sentences.daily: I open my shop at 8 AM every day.
sentences.office: Please open the meeting with introductions.
sentences.interview: I am open to new opportunities.
sentences.business: We opened a new branch in Mumbai.
sentences.formal: The museum opens at 10 AM.
sentences.informal: Open your heart — tell me what's wrong!

words.simpleMeaning: to shut or end something

sentences.daily: Please close the door when you leave.
sentences.office: We close the deal by end of month.
sentences.interview: My open-door policy means I'm rarely 'closed'.
sentences.business: We closed the financial year with 25% growth.
sentences.formal: The bank closes at 5 PM on weekdays.
sentences.informal: Close your eyes and take a deep breath.

words.usageNote: 'Close' (verb) = /kloʊz/. 'Close' (adjective, meaning near) = /kloʊs/. Different pronunciation!

words.simpleMeaning: to begin something

sentences.daily: I start my day with yoga and meditation.
sentences.office: The meeting starts at 9 AM sharp.
sentences.interview: I started this business from zero.
sentences.business: We start every project with a kickoff call.
sentences.formal: The school year starts in June.
sentences.informal: OK, let's start — no more delays!

words.simpleMeaning: to complete or end something

sentences.daily: I finish all my work before leaving.
sentences.office: We need to finish the project by Friday.
sentences.interview: I always finish what I start.
sentences.business: We finished Q3 with 32% revenue growth.
sentences.formal: Students must finish the exam in 3 hours.
sentences.informal: Are you done? Finish your food first!

words.usageNote: 'Finish' + -ing: 'She finished eating'. 'Complete' is more formal. Both mean 'to end an activity'.

words.simpleMeaning: to no longer do something or to prevent movement

sentences.daily: The bus stops near my house.
sentences.office: Stop wasting time — let's focus.
sentences.interview: I stopped my old habit of procrastinating.
sentences.business: We need to stop overspending this quarter.
sentences.formal: The police stopped the speeding car.
sentences.informal: Stop! You'll hurt yourself!

words.usageNote: 'Stop to do' = stop in order to do. 'Stop doing' = quit doing. Two very different meanings!

words.simpleMeaning: to take part in a game or to produce music

sentences.daily: I play cricket every Sunday.
sentences.office: We play an important role in the industry.
sentences.interview: I play well under pressure.
sentences.business: Play your strengths in the market.
sentences.formal: Children play to learn and develop.
sentences.informal: Come on — let's play! It's fun!

words.simpleMeaning: to let someone see or to demonstrate something

sentences.daily: Show me your results from yesterday.
sentences.office: This chart shows our monthly growth.
sentences.interview: Let me show you my portfolio.
sentences.business: Our data shows a positive trend.
sentences.formal: The teacher shows the correct method.
sentences.informal: Show me what you've got!

words.simpleMeaning: to give information to someone in words

sentences.daily: Tell me your name, please.
sentences.office: Please tell the client the project status.
sentences.interview: Tell me about yourself — classic interview opener.
sentences.business: We tell our investors the truth always.
sentences.formal: The law tells you what is allowed.
sentences.informal: Tell me — what's really going on?

words.usageNote: Irregular: tell → told → told. 'Tell' = give info. 'Say' = utter words. 'Tell someone something'.

words.simpleMeaning: to say a question or request something

sentences.daily: I ask questions when I don't understand.
sentences.office: Ask the manager for approval first.
sentences.interview: Don't hesitate to ask for clarification.
sentences.business: Our team always asks for customer feedback.
sentences.formal: Ask your doctor before taking medicine.
sentences.informal: Just ask! There are no stupid questions.

words.simpleMeaning: to reply to a question or respond to a call

sentences.daily: I answer all messages within one hour.
sentences.office: Please answer the client's email first.
sentences.interview: I can answer any question about my work.
sentences.business: We always answer customer queries promptly.
sentences.formal: The witness answers questions in court.
sentences.informal: Answer the phone — it might be important!

words.simpleMeaning: to discover or locate something

sentences.daily: I can't find my keys anywhere!
sentences.office: We need to find a solution quickly.
sentences.interview: I always find ways to solve problems.
sentences.business: We found a 20% cost reduction opportunity.
sentences.formal: Scientists find new species every year.
sentences.informal: I found the best pizza place ever!

words.usageNote: Irregular: find → found → found. 'Find' = locate. 'Find out' = discover information.

words.simpleMeaning: to continue doing something or to not give away

sentences.daily: Keep your promises — always.
sentences.office: Keep the client updated every Friday.
sentences.interview: I keep working even under pressure.
sentences.business: We keep client data fully confidential.
sentences.formal: Keep public places clean and tidy.
sentences.informal: Just keep smiling — it helps!

words.usageNote: Irregular: keep → kept → kept. 'Keep doing' = continue. 'Keep something' = retain possession.

words.simpleMeaning: to carry or transport something toward the speaker

sentences.daily: Bring your ID card to the exam.
sentences.office: Please bring the documents to the meeting.
sentences.interview: Always bring energy and enthusiasm to interviews.
sentences.business: Bring your best ideas to the table.
sentences.formal: Bring your valid ID to the polling booth.
sentences.informal: Bring me tea? Please? Pretty please?

words.usageNote: Irregular: bring → brought → brought. 'Bring' = toward speaker. 'Take' = away from speaker.

words.simpleMeaning: to go away from a place or to let something remain

sentences.daily: I leave home at 8 AM every day.
sentences.office: Please don't leave the meeting early.
sentences.interview: I leave my comfort zone to grow.
sentences.business: We never leave a client without a solution.
sentences.formal: Leave this place clean for the next person.
sentences.informal: Don't leave — the party just started!

words.usageNote: Irregular: leave → left → left. 'Leave' = depart OR let something remain.

words.simpleMeaning: to rest with eyes closed and be unconscious

sentences.daily: I sleep for 7-8 hours every night.
sentences.office: I sleep better when I exercise.
sentences.interview: I make sure I sleep well before important events.
sentences.business: Good sleep improves productivity at work.
sentences.formal: The body needs adequate sleep to function.
sentences.informal: I could sleep for a year right now!

words.usageNote: Irregular: sleep → slept → slept. 'Sleep' as noun: 'I need some sleep.'

words.simpleMeaning: to stop sleeping or to make someone stop sleeping

sentences.daily: I wake up at 6 AM every day.
sentences.office: Please wake me up before the 9 AM call.
sentences.interview: I woke up early for the interview.
sentences.business: Early birds wake up early and stay ahead.
sentences.formal: Wake up — it's time for school!
sentences.informal: Wake up! You're late again!

words.usageNote: Irregular: wake → woke → woken. 'Wake up' (phrasal) = stop sleeping.

words.simpleMeaning: to be in a position where your bottom is on a surface

sentences.daily: Please sit down and make yourself comfortable.
sentences.office: Sit in the waiting area — HR will call you.
sentences.interview: I sat at the front during the orientation.
sentences.business: We sit together to discuss strategy.
sentences.formal: Sit straight — good posture matters.
sentences.informal: Come, sit — I'll make you tea!

words.simpleMeaning: to be in an upright position on your feet

sentences.daily: I stand for 20 minutes every hour.
sentences.office: Please stand when the national anthem plays.
sentences.interview: I stand by my decisions confidently.
sentences.business: We stand behind the quality of our products.
sentences.formal: Stand up for your rights.
sentences.informal: I can't stand waiting in long queues!

words.simpleMeaning: to move at a normal pace on foot

sentences.daily: I walk 10,000 steps every day.
sentences.office: She walks to the office — it's nearby.
sentences.interview: I walked into the interview confidently.
sentences.business: Walking meetings are popular in tech companies.
sentences.formal: Walk on the left side of the footpath.
sentences.informal: Let's walk — I need some fresh air.

words.simpleMeaning: to operate a vehicle or to motivate action

sentences.daily: I drive to work every day.
sentences.office: She drives the business forward with great leadership.
sentences.interview: I drive results through hard work.
sentences.business: Innovation drives our company's growth.
sentences.formal: Drive on the left side in India.
sentences.informal: I'll drive — you relax this time.

words.simpleMeaning: to go from one place to another, especially over long distances

sentences.daily: I travel to Bangalore for conferences.
sentences.office: Our team travels to client sites monthly.
sentences.interview: I love to travel for work and leisure.
sentences.business: We travel internationally for business deals.
sentences.formal: Millions travel during festival seasons.
sentences.informal: I want to travel the whole world!

words.simpleMeaning: to learn about a subject through books or courses

sentences.daily: I study English for one hour every day.
sentences.office: I study industry trends to stay updated.
sentences.interview: I studied Computer Science from IIT.
sentences.business: Study the market before launching a product.
sentences.formal: Students study hard for competitive exams.
sentences.informal: Let's study together — it's more fun!

words.simpleMeaning: to give lessons or help someone learn

sentences.daily: My mother teaches English at a school.
sentences.office: I teach new employees the company process.
sentences.interview: I can teach and mentor junior team members.
sentences.business: Our training team teaches product skills.
sentences.formal: Teachers teach values along with subjects.
sentences.informal: Teach me your secret — you're amazing!

words.usageNote: Irregular: teach → taught → taught. 'Teach someone something' OR 'teach someone how to do'.

words.simpleMeaning: to make something free from dirt or mess

sentences.daily: I clean my room every Sunday.
sentences.office: We clean the office before clients arrive.
sentences.interview: I keep my workspace clean and organized.
sentences.business: Clean processes lead to clean results.
sentences.formal: Keep the environment clean for future generations.
sentences.informal: Clean your room — it looks like a tornado hit!

words.simpleMeaning: to clean with water and soap

sentences.daily: Wash your hands before every meal.
sentences.office: We wash our company vehicles weekly.
sentences.interview: Personal hygiene — always wash hands first.
sentences.business: Wash the equipment after every use.
sentences.formal: Wash hands for at least 20 seconds.
sentences.informal: Did you wash the dishes? No? Again?

words.simpleMeaning: to prepare food using heat

sentences.daily: My mother cooks amazing food every day.
sentences.office: We cooked a team dinner on Friday.
sentences.interview: I can cook — it shows I'm self-sufficient.
sentences.business: Our cafeteria cooks fresh meals daily.
sentences.formal: Cook vegetables properly to preserve nutrients.
sentences.informal: I cook biryani better than any restaurant!

words.simpleMeaning: to have clothes or accessories on your body

sentences.daily: I wear formal clothes to the office.
sentences.office: Please wear your ID badge at all times.
sentences.interview: I wore my best suit to the interview.
sentences.business: Wear professional attire to client meetings.
sentences.formal: Wear a helmet while riding a motorcycle.
sentences.informal: She wears her confidence like a crown!

words.usageNote: Irregular: wear → wore → worn. 'Wear' = currently having on. 'Put on' = action of wearing.

words.simpleMeaning: to make something go from one place to another

sentences.daily: I will send you the details by email.
sentences.office: Send the invoice to the client today.
sentences.interview: I always send follow-up emails after meetings.
sentences.business: Send the quarterly report to all stakeholders.
sentences.formal: Send your documents to the nearest branch.
sentences.informal: Send me a message — I miss talking to you!

words.usageNote: Irregular: send → sent → sent. 'Send someone something' = 'send something to someone'.

words.simpleMeaning: to give money in exchange for goods or services

sentences.daily: I pay my bills on time every month.
sentences.office: The company pays salaries on the 1st.
sentences.interview: I pay attention to every detail in my work.
sentences.business: We pay our vendors within 30 days.
sentences.formal: Pay your taxes on time — it's your duty.
sentences.informal: I'll pay this time — you paid last!

words.usageNote: Irregular: pay → paid → paid. 'Pay attention' = focus. 'Pay for' = bear the cost.

words.simpleMeaning: to keep safe from danger or to store money for future

sentences.daily: I save money for future goals.
sentences.office: Save the document before closing it.
sentences.interview: I save time by automating repetitive tasks.
sentences.business: We save 15% of revenue for R&D.
sentences.formal: Save water — it is precious.
sentences.informal: You saved my life — thank you so much!

words.simpleMeaning: to use money or time on something

sentences.daily: I spend weekends with my family.
sentences.office: How much does the company spend on marketing?
sentences.interview: I spend time improving my skills daily.
sentences.business: We spend ₹2 crore per year on advertising.
sentences.formal: Spend your money wisely — plan your finances.
sentences.informal: I spend way too much on online shopping!

words.usageNote: Irregular: spend → spent → spent. 'Spend time' + -ing: 'She spends time reading'.

words.simpleMeaning: to have your home somewhere or to be alive

sentences.daily: I live in Noida with my family.
sentences.office: How far do you live from the office?
sentences.interview: I have lived in 3 different cities.
sentences.business: We live by our company values every day.
sentences.formal: Every citizen has the right to live with dignity.
sentences.informal: Live in the moment — stop overthinking!

words.simpleMeaning: to change position or location

sentences.daily: I will move to Bangalore next year.
sentences.office: The company is moving to a new office.
sentences.interview: I can move quickly when there is a deadline.
sentences.business: We moved our operations online last year.
sentences.formal: Move to the side — let others pass.
sentences.informal: Move on — it's not worth your time!

words.simpleMeaning: to stay in one place until something happens

sentences.daily: I wait 5 minutes extra — I am always early.
sentences.office: Wait for the client to speak first.
sentences.interview: The opportunity I waited for has arrived.
sentences.business: Don't make clients wait — respect their time.
sentences.formal: Wait your turn in the queue.
sentences.informal: I can't wait for the weekend!

words.simpleMeaning: to have information in your memory

sentences.daily: I remember every client's name.
sentences.office: Remember to send the project update by 5 PM.
sentences.interview: Always remember to bring your portfolio.
sentences.business: Remember our company's mission in everything you do.
sentences.formal: Remember the lessons of history.
sentences.informal: I still remember the first day we met!

words.simpleMeaning: to fail to remember or not think about something

sentences.daily: I sometimes forget names — I write them down.
sentences.office: Don't forget to back up your files.
sentences.interview: I never forget a client's requirement.
sentences.business: Don't forget to submit the expense report.
sentences.formal: Forget the past — focus on the future.
sentences.informal: Don't forget to eat! You work too hard.

words.usageNote: Irregular: forget → forgot → forgotten. 'Forget to do' (slip) vs 'forget doing' (memory of event).

words.simpleMeaning: to think something is true or real

sentences.daily: I believe I can achieve anything I set my mind to.
sentences.office: We believe in delivering excellence.
sentences.interview: I believe in continuous self-improvement.
sentences.business: We believe innovation is the key to growth.
sentences.formal: Believe in justice and equality.
sentences.informal: I believe in you — you can do this!

words.usageNote: State verb — often without continuous form: 'I believe' NOT 'I am believing'. But exceptions exist.

words.simpleMeaning: to experience an emotion or physical sensation

sentences.daily: I feel happy when I help others.
sentences.office: How do you feel about the new project?
sentences.interview: I feel confident about this opportunity.
sentences.business: We feel responsible for our clients' success.
sentences.formal: Citizens feel safe when laws are enforced.
sentences.informal: I feel like I can conquer the world today!

words.simpleMeaning: to have a strong affection for or really like something

sentences.daily: I love learning new things every day.
sentences.office: We love building long-term client relationships.
sentences.interview: I love working in a team environment.
sentences.business: Our company loves disrupting traditional markets.
sentences.formal: Love your neighbor as yourself.
sentences.informal: I love mangoes more than any other fruit!

words.simpleMeaning: to enjoy or find something pleasant

sentences.daily: I like reading books in my free time.
sentences.office: Do you like the new office design?
sentences.interview: I like to learn from every experience.
sentences.business: Our clients like our prompt response time.
sentences.formal: We like clean air and clean water.
sentences.informal: I like cats more than dogs — fight me!

words.simpleMeaning: to dislike something very strongly

sentences.daily: I hate being late — so I'm always early.
sentences.office: We hate excuses — we focus on solutions.
sentences.interview: I hate procrastinating — it kills productivity.
sentences.business: We hate customer complaints — so we prevent them.
sentences.formal: We must hate injustice and speak against it.
sentences.informal: I hate Mondays. There, I said it.

words.simpleMeaning: to want something to happen and think it's possible

sentences.daily: I hope to get a promotion this year.
sentences.office: I hope this project is successful.
sentences.interview: I hope to contribute meaningfully to your team.
sentences.business: We hope to achieve record sales this year.
sentences.formal: We hope for a peaceful society.
sentences.informal: I hope you have the best day ever!

words.simpleMeaning: to decide what you are going to do

sentences.daily: I plan my day the night before.
sentences.office: We plan the quarter in advance.
sentences.interview: I planned every detail of the interview preparation.
sentences.business: We plan our marketing campaigns 3 months ahead.
sentences.formal: Plan your career — don't just let it happen.
sentences.informal: I plan to eat healthy. Tomorrow. Maybe.

words.simpleMeaning: to occur or take place

sentences.daily: What happened yesterday? Tell me everything.
sentences.office: This never happens in a well-managed project.
sentences.interview: If you don't prepare, what happens at the interview?
sentences.business: This happens when teams don't communicate.
sentences.formal: Accidents happen — that's why we have safety rules.
sentences.informal: Whatever happens, we'll get through it together!

words.simpleMeaning: to become or make different

sentences.daily: I change my approach based on feedback.
sentences.office: We changed the project deadline by one week.
sentences.interview: I change and adapt — that's my strength.
sentences.business: We changed our pricing model last quarter.
sentences.formal: Change your mindset — change your life.
sentences.informal: Don't change who you are for anyone!

words.simpleMeaning: to examine something to make sure it is correct

sentences.daily: Always check your work before submitting.
sentences.office: Please check the email before hitting send.
sentences.interview: I always check my answers twice.
sentences.business: We check quality at every stage of production.
sentences.formal: Check your vehicle before a long journey.
sentences.informal: Check your phone — I sent something funny!

words.simpleMeaning: to pick one option from several

sentences.daily: Choose your words carefully in interviews.
sentences.office: Choose the best option from the list.
sentences.interview: I choose to be positive every day.
sentences.business: We choose partners based on shared values.
sentences.formal: Choose leaders who truly represent the people.
sentences.informal: You can choose to be happy — choose it!

words.usageNote: Irregular: choose → chose → chosen. 'Choose' = present. 'Chose' = past.

words.simpleMeaning: to make a choice or judgment

sentences.daily: I decide my daily priorities every morning.
sentences.office: Management will decide the budget next week.
sentences.interview: I have decided to pursue this career path.
sentences.business: We decided to pivot our business model.
sentences.formal: Voters decide who leads the country.
sentences.informal: Decide already — the menu is not that hard!

words.simpleMeaning: to make or produce something new

sentences.daily: I create a to-do list every morning.
sentences.office: We create innovative solutions for clients.
sentences.interview: I have created several successful projects.
sentences.business: We create value for all stakeholders.
sentences.formal: Art teachers create an environment of creativity.
sentences.informal: I create magic in the kitchen!

words.simpleMeaning: to construct something or develop something over time

sentences.daily: I build good habits every day.
sentences.office: We are building a new product from scratch.
sentences.interview: I have built a strong professional network.
sentences.business: We build technology that solves real problems.
sentences.formal: Build your skills — no one can take them away.
sentences.informal: Build your dreams or someone will hire you to build theirs!

words.simpleMeaning: to be the best or most successful in a competition

sentences.daily: I win when I stay focused and disciplined.
sentences.office: Our team won the best performance award.
sentences.interview: I always play to win — ethically.
sentences.business: We won the bid for a ₹5 crore project.
sentences.formal: Win the trust of the people through good governance.
sentences.informal: We won! I can't believe it!

words.usageNote: Irregular: win → won → won. 'Win at something' or 'win something'. Not 'win to someone'.

words.simpleMeaning: to be defeated or to no longer have something

sentences.daily: I don't lose — I learn.
sentences.office: We cannot afford to lose this client.
sentences.interview: I lose focus when I am not organized.
sentences.business: Losing a deal teaches us what to improve.
sentences.formal: Losing hope is worse than losing a battle.
sentences.informal: I always lose my keys — it's my superpower.

words.usageNote: Irregular: lose → lost → lost. 'Loose' (adjective, means not tight) is often confused with 'lose'.

words.simpleMeaning: to increase in size, number, or importance

sentences.daily: I grow mentally every day through reading.
sentences.office: Our business grows 20% year over year.
sentences.interview: I have grown enormously in my career.
sentences.business: We plan to grow into international markets.
sentences.formal: Grow trees — they give us life.
sentences.informal: You've grown so much — I'm proud of you!

words.usageNote: Irregular: grow → grew → grown. 'Grow up' = become adult. 'Grow' = increase in general.

words.simpleMeaning: to gain knowledge or skill — revisit for professional collocations

sentences.daily: I learn one new English word every day.
sentences.office: We learn from every project we complete.
sentences.interview: I am always learning to stay relevant.
sentences.business: Learning is embedded in our company culture.
sentences.formal: Learn from the past to build a better future.
sentences.informal: I'm always learning — even from failures!

words.simpleMeaning: to make or become better

sentences.daily: I improve my English a little every day.
sentences.office: We constantly improve our services.
sentences.interview: I improved my score from 60 to 95.
sentences.business: Improving customer experience is our top priority.
sentences.formal: Improve your health by eating better.
sentences.informal: I'm improving every day — watch this space!

words.simpleMeaning: to do something regularly to get better

sentences.daily: I practice speaking English every day.
sentences.office: We practice presenting before client meetings.
sentences.interview: I practiced for 3 months before the interview.
sentences.business: Practice makes a business team world-class.
sentences.formal: Practice good habits daily.
sentences.informal: Practice till you can't get it wrong!

words.simpleMeaning: to make something or someone ready

sentences.daily: I prepare my schedule the night before.
sentences.office: Please prepare the presentation for tomorrow.
sentences.interview: I always prepare thoroughly for every interview.
sentences.business: We prepare customized proposals for each client.
sentences.formal: Prepare for emergencies before they happen.
sentences.informal: I'll prepare dinner — you just relax!

words.simpleMeaning: to finish something fully

sentences.daily: I complete all tasks before their deadline.
sentences.office: We need to complete the report by end of day.
sentences.interview: I completed my MBA from Delhi University.
sentences.business: We completed the project 2 weeks ahead of schedule.
sentences.formal: Complete your education — it opens all doors.
sentences.informal: Done! Project complete — let's celebrate!

words.simpleMeaning: to become a member or to connect things together

sentences.daily: I will join a yoga class next month.
sentences.office: I joined this company three years ago.
sentences.interview: Why do you want to join our organization?
sentences.business: Join our platform and grow your business.
sentences.formal: Join the movement for clean India.
sentences.informal: Join us! The more the merrier!

words.simpleMeaning: to give part of something to others or to have together

sentences.daily: I share my knowledge with my team.
sentences.office: Share the file on Google Drive, please.
sentences.interview: I am happy to share my experience.
sentences.business: We share resources across all departments.
sentences.formal: Share and care — it builds society.
sentences.informal: Share this post — help me go viral!

words.simpleMeaning: to get pleasure from something

sentences.daily: I enjoy learning new languages.
sentences.office: We enjoy collaborating with creative clients.
sentences.interview: I enjoy solving complex problems.
sentences.business: Our employees enjoy flexible working hours.
sentences.formal: Enjoy nature — protect it for future generations.
sentences.informal: I enjoy every second of this weekend!

words.simpleMeaning: to make something clear or easy to understand

sentences.daily: Please explain the instructions clearly.
sentences.office: Can you explain the billing process?
sentences.interview: I can explain complex ideas in simple terms.
sentences.business: Explain the features to the client clearly.
sentences.formal: Explain your rights to citizens.
sentences.informal: Let me explain — you misunderstood me!

words.simpleMeaning: to say what something or someone is like

sentences.daily: Describe yourself in three words.
sentences.office: Please describe the problem in detail.
sentences.interview: Describe your biggest achievement.
sentences.business: Describe the product clearly to customers.
sentences.formal: Describe your symptoms to the doctor.
sentences.informal: How would you describe this food? Pure bliss!

words.simpleMeaning: to talk about something with others

sentences.daily: Let's discuss this over coffee.
sentences.office: We will discuss the budget in today's meeting.
sentences.interview: I love to discuss ideas openly.
sentences.business: We discuss strategy with all team leads.
sentences.formal: Discuss problems openly in a democracy.
sentences.informal: We need to discuss something important. Now.

words.simpleMeaning: to choose after thinking about possibilities

sentences.daily: Decide what you want — then go get it.
sentences.office: The board will decide by next Friday.
sentences.interview: I decided to take the promotion in Bangalore.
sentences.business: We decide based on data — not gut feelings.
sentences.formal: Let the people decide who leads them.
sentences.informal: Just decide — you've been thinking for 2 hours!

words.simpleMeaning: to agree to take or receive something

sentences.daily: I accept my mistakes and learn from them.
sentences.office: The company accepted our proposal.
sentences.interview: I accept this offer with great enthusiasm.
sentences.business: We accept payments in all formats.
sentences.formal: Accept criticism gracefully — it helps you grow.
sentences.informal: I accept! Let's do this!

words.simpleMeaning: to stay away from or not do something

sentences.daily: Avoid eating junk food regularly.
sentences.office: Avoid checking social media during work hours.
sentences.interview: I avoid vague answers in interviews.
sentences.business: We avoid overpromising and underdelivering.
sentences.formal: Avoid plastic to protect the environment.
sentences.informal: Avoid that shop — everything is overpriced!

words.simpleMeaning: to control or organize something successfully

sentences.daily: I manage my time using a planner.
sentences.office: She manages a team of 25 people.
sentences.interview: Can you manage multiple projects at once?
sentences.business: We manage ₹50 crore in client investments.
sentences.formal: Good leaders manage with empathy.
sentences.informal: I can manage — don't worry about me!

words.simpleMeaning: to help or encourage someone or something

sentences.daily: My family supports me in everything.
sentences.office: We support employees in their career growth.
sentences.interview: I support every teammate who needs help.
sentences.business: Our company supports 500 small businesses.
sentences.formal: A government must support its citizens.
sentences.informal: I support you 100% — always!

words.simpleMeaning: to successfully reach a goal

sentences.daily: I achieve my goals through consistent effort.
sentences.office: We achieved record profits last year.
sentences.interview: I believe I can achieve anything I set my mind to.
sentences.business: We help clients achieve their business goals.
sentences.formal: Achieve greatness through small daily improvements.
sentences.informal: You achieved it! I always knew you would!

words.simpleMeaning: to find an answer to a problem

sentences.daily: I solve problems quickly and calmly.
sentences.office: Let's solve this issue before the client notices.
sentences.interview: I am a problem-solver by nature.
sentences.business: We solve complex business challenges daily.
sentences.formal: Solve community problems with creative thinking.
sentences.informal: Solved! And it only took 5 minutes!

words.simpleMeaning: to link things or people together

sentences.daily: Connect the charger — the phone is dead.
sentences.office: Connect me with the person in charge.
sentences.interview: I connect with professionals on LinkedIn.
sentences.business: We connect buyers with the right suppliers.
sentences.formal: Connect with your roots — know your culture.
sentences.informal: Connect your laptop to the projector first!

words.simpleMeaning: to go into a place or to type information

sentences.daily: Enter through the main gate only.
sentences.office: Enter your password and click login.
sentences.interview: I entered the room confidently for the interview.
sentences.business: Enter your company details on the registration form.
sentences.formal: Enter the hall quietly during the ceremony.
sentences.informal: Enter your name here and you're done!

words.simpleMeaning: to write using a keyboard

sentences.daily: I type 60 words per minute.
sentences.office: Please type your query in the search box.
sentences.interview: Type your responses clearly in the interview form.
sentences.business: Type the client's feedback into the CRM.
sentences.formal: Type your address to find the nearest service center.
sentences.informal: Can you type faster? This takes forever!

words.simpleMeaning: to copy files from the internet to your device

sentences.daily: Download the app from the Play Store.
sentences.office: Please download and sign the contract.
sentences.interview: I downloaded all the resources for my prep.
sentences.business: Download the client report and review it.
sentences.formal: Download only from trusted websites.
sentences.informal: Why does this download take forever?

words.simpleMeaning: to send files from your device to the internet

sentences.daily: Upload your ID proof to the portal.
sentences.office: Upload the presentation before the meeting.
sentences.interview: I uploaded my resume to the job portal.
sentences.business: Upload product images in high resolution.
sentences.formal: Upload only clean and safe content.
sentences.informal: My internet is slow — the upload will take ages!

words.simpleMeaning: to look for something carefully

sentences.daily: Search for the best route on Google Maps.
sentences.office: Search the client database before calling.
sentences.interview: I searched for the best preparation resources.
sentences.business: We search for talent across all channels.
sentences.formal: Search for truth before forming opinions.
sentences.informal: I searched everywhere — where are my glasses?

words.simpleMeaning: to give a formal account of something

sentences.daily: I report my progress to my manager weekly.
sentences.office: Report any bug to the technical team immediately.
sentences.interview: I reported my achievements clearly in the interview.
sentences.business: Report monthly sales figures to the CEO.
sentences.formal: Report corruption — it is your civic duty.
sentences.informal: I need to report something important. Listen up!

words.simpleMeaning: to come into view or to seem

sentences.daily: She appears confident in every situation.
sentences.office: Please appear for the interview on time.
sentences.interview: I appeared for 5 interviews last month.
sentences.business: The new product will appear in stores by December.
sentences.formal: Appear presentable in public at all times.
sentences.informal: You appear tired — go rest a bit!

words.simpleMeaning: to keep doing something without stopping

sentences.daily: Continue reading — the best part is coming.
sentences.office: We will continue this discussion tomorrow.
sentences.interview: I continued working even when things were hard.
sentences.business: Continue investing in your team's growth.
sentences.formal: Continue our traditions — they define us.
sentences.informal: Don't stop! Continue — you're almost there!

words.simpleMeaning: to come or go back

sentences.daily: I return home by 7 PM every evening.
sentences.office: Return the signed contract at your earliest.
sentences.interview: I return calls within 2 hours — always.
sentences.business: We return products within 7 days if faulty.
sentences.formal: Return to your motherland — it needs you.
sentences.informal: Return the book — someone else needs it too!

words.simpleMeaning: to make or become larger in amount or size

sentences.daily: I increase my reading speed by 10% per month.
sentences.office: The company increased salaries by 15%.
sentences.interview: I want to increase my value to the organization.
sentences.business: We increased revenue by 40% this quarter.
sentences.formal: We must increase investment in education.
sentences.informal: My confidence has increased so much this year!

words.simpleMeaning: to make smaller in amount, size, or degree

sentences.daily: I try to reduce screen time after 10 PM.
sentences.office: We need to reduce costs by 10%.
sentences.interview: I reduced my preparation time using smart methods.
sentences.business: We reduced delivery time from 7 days to 3.
sentences.formal: Reduce waste to protect the environment.
sentences.informal: I need to reduce my caffeine intake. Someday.

words.simpleMeaning: to arrive somewhere or to achieve something

sentences.daily: I reach the office 10 minutes early daily.
sentences.office: We will reach the target by March.
sentences.interview: I reached the interview venue 30 minutes early.
sentences.business: We reached 1 million users last month!
sentences.formal: Reach for your goals — never settle.
sentences.informal: I reached my destination! Finally!

words.simpleMeaning: to not succeed or not achieve what was intended

sentences.daily: It's okay to fail — just get back up.
sentences.office: Don't fail the client — deliver what you promise.
sentences.interview: I failed my first interview but learned a lot.
sentences.business: Failing once doesn't mean you failed forever.
sentences.formal: Failure is the first step to success.
sentences.informal: Fail forward — every failure brings a lesson!

words.simpleMeaning: to achieve a goal or do well

sentences.daily: I succeed because I never give up.
sentences.office: Our company has succeeded in 12 markets.
sentences.interview: I want to succeed in this role.
sentences.business: Succeed by being consistent and smart.
sentences.formal: When you work hard, you will succeed.
sentences.informal: You succeeded! I always believed in you!

words.simpleMeaning: to say you will provide or do something

sentences.daily: I can offer help whenever you need it.
sentences.office: We offer a 30-day free trial.
sentences.interview: I was offered the position right after the interview.
sentences.business: We offer the best after-sales service.
sentences.formal: The job offer came with great benefits.
sentences.informal: I'll offer to help — just say the word!

words.simpleMeaning: to have the same opinion as someone

sentences.daily: I totally agree with your point.
sentences.office: Do you agree to the terms and conditions?
sentences.interview: I agree — this is the right approach.
sentences.business: We agreed on the terms after 3 meetings.
sentences.formal: Citizens must agree to follow the law.
sentences.informal: Wow, I actually agree with you! Rare!

words.simpleMeaning: first-person singular present of 'be'

sentences.daily: I am a student of English.
sentences.office: I am the project manager for this account.
sentences.interview: I am extremely passionate about learning.
sentences.business: I am the founder of this startup.
sentences.formal: Every citizen is who they are.
sentences.informal: I am so happy to be here!

words.usageNote: "Am" = first person singular only. Always use 'I am'. Never 'I is' or 'I are'.

words.simpleMeaning: third-person singular present of 'be'

sentences.daily: He is a very good teacher.
sentences.office: The meeting is at 3 PM today.
sentences.interview: This is the best opportunity of my career.
sentences.business: The market is growing at 15% annually.
sentences.formal: Democracy is the voice of the people.
sentences.informal: This is the best day ever!

words.usageNote: "Is" = He/She/It/singular noun. Never 'He are' or 'She am'.

words.simpleMeaning: plural or second-person present of 'be'

sentences.daily: We are a team of passionate learners.
sentences.office: These are the project deliverables.
sentences.interview: You are exactly what we are looking for.
sentences.business: We are the market leaders in EdTech.
sentences.formal: Citizens are the backbone of democracy.
sentences.informal: You are amazing — never forget that!

words.usageNote: "Are" = You/We/They/plural nouns. 'Is' is singular, 'are' is plural.

words.simpleMeaning: past tense of 'be' for I/he/she/it

sentences.daily: I was a student at Delhi University.
sentences.office: Yesterday's meeting was very productive.
sentences.interview: I was nervous before the interview.
sentences.business: The company was founded in 2010.
sentences.formal: The past was different — we must move forward.
sentences.informal: That was the best biryani of my life!

words.usageNote: "Was" = I/He/She/It + past. Irregular past of 'be'.

words.simpleMeaning: past tense of 'be' for you/we/they

sentences.daily: We were classmates in college.
sentences.office: There were 50 people at the conference.
sentences.interview: They were very impressed with my performance.
sentences.business: Revenues were higher than expected last year.
sentences.formal: Those were the golden days of our country.
sentences.informal: Those were the best years of my life!

words.usageNote: "Were" = You/We/They + past. Also used for unreal conditions: 'If I were you...'

words.simpleMeaning: to start to be something different

sentences.daily: I want to become a fluent English speaker.
sentences.office: She has become the team leader.
sentences.interview: I want to become a confident communicator.
sentences.business: Our startup has become a market leader.
sentences.formal: We must all become better citizens.
sentences.informal: You became exactly who I knew you would!

words.usageNote: Irregular: become → became → become. 'Become + noun/adjective': 'He became a doctor.'

words.simpleMeaning: to appear or give the impression of being something

sentences.daily: You seem tired today — get some rest.
sentences.office: This plan seems feasible to me.
sentences.interview: I seem calm but I was nervous inside.
sentences.business: The market seems to be improving.
sentences.formal: Things are not always what they seem.
sentences.informal: It seems like it's going to rain!

words.usageNote: "Seem" is a state verb. 'It seems' = It appears. Followed by adjective or 'to be + noun'.

words.simpleMeaning: to have a particular appearance

sentences.daily: You look great in that formal shirt!
sentences.office: The project looks on track.
sentences.interview: I look confident — even when I'm not.
sentences.business: This quarter looks very promising.
sentences.formal: Things look better in the morning.
sentences.informal: You look like you need a vacation!

words.simpleMeaning: to seem or be perceived a certain way

sentences.daily: I feel confident about tomorrow.
sentences.office: The office feels warm and welcoming.
sentences.interview: I felt ready for every question asked.
sentences.business: The brand feels premium and trustworthy.
sentences.formal: Freedom feels like breathing fresh air.
sentences.informal: I feel like a superhero today!

words.simpleMeaning: to seem a certain way when heard or described

sentences.daily: That sounds like a great idea!
sentences.office: Your proposal sounds very promising.
sentences.interview: I want to sound confident and clear.
