sentences.interview: Walk slowly — the interview starts in 10 minutes.
sentences.business: Slowly we expanded from 1 to 12 cities.
sentences.formal: Change comes slowly — but it comes.
sentences.informal: Slowly getting better — and that's enough!

words.simpleMeaning: with great attention to avoid mistakes

sentences.daily: Read each question carefully before answering.
sentences.office: Listen carefully to what the client says.
sentences.interview: I carefully prepared each answer beforehand.
sentences.business: We carefully review every contract.
sentences.formal: Choose your leaders carefully — it matters.
sentences.informal: Read carefully. Think carefully. Speak carefully. Win!

words.simpleMeaning: with complete accuracy; in every detail

sentences.daily: That's exactly what I wanted to say!
sentences.office: You are exactly on time — respect!
sentences.interview: I answered exactly as I had prepared.
sentences.business: That's exactly the problem we solve.
sentences.formal: Justice means exactly the same treatment for all.
sentences.informal: Exactly what I thought — great minds!

words.hindi: ख़ास तौर पर / विशेष रूप से
words.simpleMeaning: more than usual or more than others

sentences.daily: I especially love learning idioms and phrases.
sentences.office: This is especially important for client-facing roles.
sentences.interview: I paid attention especially to grammar questions.
sentences.business: We focus especially on small business clients.
sentences.formal: Minorities must be protected especially in a democracy.
sentences.informal: You are especially talented — stand in your power!

words.simpleMeaning: in a clear manner; easy to see or understand

sentences.daily: Speak clearly — your ideas deserve to be heard.
sentences.office: Explain the project status clearly in the email.
sentences.interview: I answered clearly and confidently.
sentences.business: Communicate value clearly to potential customers.
sentences.formal: Speak clearly — citizens deserve to understand their leaders.
sentences.informal: Speak clearly. Think clearly. Win clearly!

words.simpleMeaning: in every way; totally

sentences.daily: I am completely committed to this journey.
sentences.office: I completely understand your concern.
sentences.interview: I was completely prepared for the interview.
sentences.business: We completely redesigned our product for 2025.
sentences.formal: Complete freedom means completely equal rights.
sentences.informal: I'm completely, totally, 100% serious about this!

words.simpleMeaning: very nearly but not quite

sentences.daily: I have almost finished reading the lesson.
sentences.office: We are almost at the 1 million user mark.
sentences.interview: I was almost selected — I will try harder.
sentences.business: We are almost ready to launch version 2.
sentences.formal: We are almost there — as a nation.
sentences.informal: Almost there! Keep going!

words.simpleMeaning: first person singular subject pronoun

sentences.daily: I am learning English every day.
sentences.office: I completed the task on time.
sentences.interview: I am the best candidate for this role.
sentences.business: I manage a team of 15 people.
sentences.formal: I exercise my right to vote.
sentences.informal: I believe in myself — and that's enough!

words.usageNote: Always capital 'I' — never lowercase 'i' when used as a pronoun. 'I' is the subject.

words.simpleMeaning: first person singular object pronoun

sentences.daily: Tell me your name, please.
sentences.office: Please give me 5 minutes to explain.
sentences.interview: They selected me for the final round.
sentences.business: Our clients trust me with their projects.
sentences.formal: Help me become a better citizen.
sentences.informal: Believe in me like I believe in myself!

words.usageNote: Object pronoun — used after verbs and prepositions. 'Give me', 'call me', 'between you and me'.

words.simpleMeaning: first person singular possessive adjective

sentences.daily: English is my priority this year.
sentences.office: My manager gave me great feedback.
sentences.interview: My biggest strength is my consistency.
sentences.business: My team achieved the quarterly target.
sentences.formal: My duty as a citizen is to vote.
sentences.informal: My journey, my rules, my success!

words.usageNote: Possessive adjective — before noun: 'my book'. Not a pronoun by itself: use 'mine'.

words.simpleMeaning: first person singular possessive pronoun

sentences.daily: This achievement is mine — earned with hard work.
sentences.office: Is this pen yours or mine?
sentences.interview: The idea was mine, and it worked!
sentences.business: This success is mine and my team's together.
sentences.formal: This land is mine and my country's.
sentences.informal: That's MINE — all mine!

words.simpleMeaning: second person subject/object pronoun

sentences.daily: You can speak English fluently with practice.
sentences.office: You are doing a great job — keep it up.
sentences.interview: Can you tell me about your experience?
sentences.business: You are our most valued client.
sentences.formal: You have the power to change the country.
sentences.informal: You are going to achieve great things — I know it!

words.simpleMeaning: second person possessive adjective

sentences.daily: Your English will improve with daily practice.
sentences.office: Please submit your report by Friday.
sentences.interview: What is your greatest achievement?
sentences.business: Your loyalty means a lot to us.
sentences.formal: Know your rights — and your duties.
sentences.informal: Your potential is limitless — believe that!

words.simpleMeaning: third person singular male subject pronoun

sentences.daily: He is a very hardworking student.
sentences.office: He manages the entire operations team.
sentences.interview: He spoke very confidently in the interview.
sentences.business: He leads the company with vision and clarity.
sentences.formal: He represents his constituency with integrity.
sentences.informal: He's going to do something amazing — mark my words!

words.simpleMeaning: third person singular male object pronoun

sentences.daily: Call him when you have time.
sentences.office: Please give him the project brief.
sentences.interview: They selected him for the leadership program.
sentences.business: Our clients trust him with their accounts.
sentences.formal: Help him — he needs your support.
sentences.informal: Tell him I said hello!

words.simpleMeaning: third person singular male possessive

sentences.daily: His English has improved so much!
sentences.office: His dedication inspired the whole team.
sentences.interview: His preparation was evident in every answer.
sentences.business: His startup raised ₹10 crore in seed funding.
sentences.formal: His leadership changed the community.
sentences.informal: His confidence makes everyone around him confident too!

words.simpleMeaning: third person singular female subject pronoun

sentences.daily: She speaks English with great confidence.
sentences.office: She heads the product development team.
sentences.interview: She answered every question brilliantly.
sentences.business: She grew the company from 5 to 500 employees.
sentences.formal: She represents her village in parliament.
sentences.informal: She is going places — no doubt about it!

words.simpleMeaning: third person singular female object/possessive

sentences.daily: Give her the feedback she deserves.
sentences.office: Her presentation won the client over.
sentences.interview: They offered her the position the same day.
sentences.business: Her leadership style is collaborative.
sentences.formal: Her rights must be protected.
sentences.informal: Her smile is the best thing in the room!

words.simpleMeaning: third person singular neutral pronoun

sentences.daily: It is a beautiful day to learn English.
sentences.office: It was a very productive meeting.
sentences.interview: It was my best interview — I felt confident.
sentences.business: It was the best product launch in our history.
sentences.formal: It is the duty of every citizen to vote.
sentences.informal: It is what it is — but we can change it!

words.simpleMeaning: first person plural subject pronoun

sentences.daily: We are learning English together.
sentences.office: We have an excellent team culture.
sentences.interview: We work best as a team.
sentences.business: We serve 10,000+ businesses globally.
sentences.formal: We are the change this country needs.
sentences.informal: We got this — together, always!

words.simpleMeaning: first person plural object pronoun

sentences.daily: English can help us get better jobs.
sentences.office: Contact us for any queries or support.
sentences.interview: The interviewer was impressed with us.
sentences.business: Our clients recommend us to their partners.
sentences.formal: Join us — together we are stronger.
sentences.informal: That's what makes us amazing — teamwork!

words.simpleMeaning: first person plural possessive adjective

sentences.daily: Our English is getting better every day!
sentences.office: Our team is the best in the company.
sentences.interview: Our company values are transparency and trust.
sentences.business: Our product is used by 500,000+ users.
sentences.formal: Our nation belongs to all of us.
sentences.informal: Our story is just beginning — and it's beautiful!

words.simpleMeaning: third person plural subject pronoun

sentences.daily: They are my classmates and study partners.
sentences.office: They are the best clients we have ever served.
sentences.interview: They were impressed by my presentation.
sentences.business: They operate in 12 countries worldwide.
sentences.formal: They voted — their voice was heard.
sentences.informal: They believed in me before I believed in myself!

words.simpleMeaning: third person plural object pronoun

sentences.daily: Call them if you need any help.
sentences.office: Please send them the presentation.
sentences.interview: I prepared to impress them from day one.
sentences.business: We sent them a thank-you note.
sentences.formal: Protect them — the vulnerable need our support.
sentences.informal: Help them — and they'll help you back tenfold!

words.simpleMeaning: third person plural possessive adjective

sentences.daily: Their English is an inspiration to me.
sentences.office: Respect their time and deadlines always.
sentences.interview: Their company has 10,000 employees globally.
sentences.business: Their feedback helped us improve by 40%.
sentences.formal: Their rights are our responsibility.
sentences.informal: Their success is our success — we grow together!

words.simpleMeaning: used to identify a specific thing or person nearby

sentences.daily: This is my first step to fluency!
sentences.office: This is the agenda for today's meeting.
sentences.interview: This is the most important interview of my life.
sentences.business: This feature is our competitive advantage.
sentences.formal: This is our chance to change things.
sentences.informal: This moment is everything — seize it!

words.simpleMeaning: used to identify a specific thing or person farther away

sentences.daily: That was the best lesson I ever had.
sentences.office: That sounds like a great idea — let's do it.
sentences.interview: That interview taught me more than a book.
sentences.business: That campaign generated ₹2 crore in revenue.
sentences.formal: That decision changed the course of history.
sentences.informal: That's exactly what I was thinking!

words.simpleMeaning: used to ask for information about something

sentences.daily: What is your name?
sentences.office: What is the status of the project?
sentences.interview: What are your strengths?
sentences.business: What is our revenue for this quarter?
sentences.formal: What can I do for my country today?
sentences.informal: What are you waiting for? Let's go!

words.simpleMeaning: used to ask about a person

sentences.daily: Who is your English teacher?
sentences.office: Who is responsible for this task?
sentences.interview: Who referred you for this position?
sentences.business: Who is our biggest competitor right now?
sentences.formal: Who will speak for the people?
sentences.informal: Who are you? Tell me everything!

words.simpleMeaning: used to ask about a place

sentences.daily: Where do you study English?
sentences.office: Where is the meeting room?
sentences.interview: Where do you see yourself in 5 years?
sentences.business: Where are we in the project timeline?
sentences.formal: Where is the voice of the people?
sentences.informal: Where do we even begin?

words.simpleMeaning: used to ask about a time

sentences.daily: When do you practice English?
sentences.office: When is the deadline for this project?
sentences.interview: When can I join if I get selected?
sentences.business: When will the new product launch?
sentences.formal: When will justice be served?
sentences.informal: When is coffee not a good idea? Never.

words.simpleMeaning: used to ask for a reason or explanation

sentences.daily: Why do you want to learn English?
sentences.office: Why was the project delayed?
sentences.interview: Why do you want to join this company?
sentences.business: Why should clients choose us over others?
sentences.formal: Why should citizens obey unjust laws?
sentences.informal: Why? Because I said so — and because it's right!

words.simpleMeaning: used to ask about the way something is done or the extent

sentences.daily: How do I improve my English speaking?
sentences.office: How can I help you today?
sentences.interview: How do you handle pressure at work?
sentences.business: How are we performing against our targets?
sentences.formal: How can we build a better society?
sentences.informal: How did you get so good? Practice!

words.simpleMeaning: used to ask about a choice between alternatives

sentences.daily: Which chapter is the most important?
sentences.office: Which option should we go with?
sentences.interview: Which role interests you the most?
sentences.business: Which strategy delivered the best ROI?
sentences.formal: Which candidate speaks for all citizens?
sentences.informal: Which is better — A or B? Tell me honestly!

words.simpleMeaning: used to ask about possession

sentences.daily: Whose notebook is this?
sentences.office: Whose responsibility is this task?
sentences.interview: Whose portfolio impressed you the most?
sentences.business: Whose department delivered the best results?
sentences.formal: Whose land is it? Every citizen's.
sentences.informal: Whose side are you on? The right one, I hope!

words.simpleMeaning: used to refer to a person as the object of a verb

sentences.daily: Whom did you consult for this advice?
sentences.office: To whom should I address the letter?
sentences.interview: Whom do you admire most in your career?
sentences.business: With whom are we competing in this market?
sentences.formal: For whom does the government work?
sentences.informal: Whom can I call at 2 AM? My best friend!

words.simpleMeaning: used to introduce a contrast or limitation

sentences.daily: English is hard — however, practice makes it easy.
sentences.office: However, we need to meet the deadline.
sentences.interview: The interview was tough; however, I stayed calm.
sentences.business: Revenue dropped; however, profits increased.
sentences.formal: However difficult it is — keep going.
sentences.informal: However — and this is important — never give up!

words.simpleMeaning: used to indicate inclusion or location inside something

sentences.daily: I live in Delhi, the capital of India.
sentences.office: The document is in the shared drive.
sentences.interview: I am confident in my English abilities.
sentences.business: We are expanding in South Asian markets.
sentences.formal: In a democracy, power lies in the people.
sentences.informal: I am all in — 100%!

words.usageNote: 'In' + country/city/large area. 'At' + specific point. 'On' + surface/day.

words.simpleMeaning: touching a surface or about a specific day/topic

sentences.daily: The book is on the table.
sentences.office: We have a meeting on Monday at 10 AM.
sentences.interview: Please focus on your daily English practice.
sentences.business: Click 'on' the button to submit the form.
sentences.formal: Every opinion must be based on facts.
sentences.informal: I'm ON it — consider it done!

words.simpleMeaning: indicating a specific point in space or time

sentences.daily: I am at my desk, ready to learn.
sentences.office: The interview is at 11 AM sharp.
sentences.interview: Look at me when I speak — it shows respect.
sentences.business: We are great at customer service.
sentences.formal: We will meet at the polling booth.
sentences.informal: I am at my best when challenged!

words.simpleMeaning: indicating direction, purpose, or the infinitive

sentences.daily: I am going to the library to study.
sentences.office: To become fluent, you must practice daily.
sentences.interview: I went to the interview fully prepared.
sentences.business: We want to grow to 10 million users.
sentences.formal: The right to education belongs to everyone.
sentences.informal: To infinity and beyond — that's my goal!

words.simpleMeaning: indicating origin, source, or starting point

sentences.daily: I am from a small town in UP.
sentences.office: This email is from the HR team.
sentences.interview: I learned from every failure I faced.
sentences.business: We ship products from our Mumbai warehouse.
sentences.formal: Freedom fighters came from every corner of India.
sentences.informal: Where are you from? Tell me your story!

words.simpleMeaning: indicating accompaniment or using something

sentences.daily: I study with a friend every evening.
sentences.office: I agree with your suggestion.
sentences.interview: I came with full preparation and confidence.
sentences.business: Work with your team — not just for them.
sentences.formal: With freedom comes responsibility.
sentences.informal: I'm with you — all the way!

words.hindi: के लिए / लंबे समय के लिए
words.simpleMeaning: indicating purpose, duration, or reason

sentences.daily: I study English for one hour every day.
sentences.office: I have been working for 3 years in IT.
sentences.interview: This opportunity is made for me.
sentences.business: We work for our clients' success.
sentences.formal: Stand for justice — always.
sentences.informal: This is for you — thank you for everything!

words.simpleMeaning: concerning or approximately

sentences.daily: Tell me about yourself.
sentences.office: We talked about the new project plan.
sentences.interview: What is this role about, exactly?
sentences.business: This is about serving our customers better.
sentences.formal: What is this government doing about poverty?
sentences.informal: Let's talk about your future — it's bright!

words.simpleMeaning: following in time or order

sentences.daily: After studying, I practice speaking.
sentences.office: Please send the report after the meeting.
sentences.interview: After the rejection, I prepared even harder.
sentences.business: After analysis, we made the right call.
sentences.formal: After every storm comes sunshine.
sentences.informal: After today, nothing will be the same — in a good way!

words.simpleMeaning: earlier in time or place

sentences.daily: I prepare before every English session.
sentences.office: Send the agenda before the meeting.
sentences.interview: Before the interview, I meditated for 5 minutes.
sentences.business: Before launch, test thoroughly.
sentences.formal: Before pointing fingers, check yourself.
sentences.informal: Before you doubt yourself — remember how far you've come!

words.simpleMeaning: in a lower position or below

sentences.daily: I work well under pressure.
sentences.office: The documents are under the folder named 'contracts'.
sentences.interview: Under the new manager, things improved.
sentences.business: We operate under a strict quality policy.
sentences.formal: Under the law, all are equal.
sentences.informal: Under pressure, I shine. Seriously!

words.simpleMeaning: above or more than

sentences.daily: I have studied over 1000 English words!
sentences.office: The project is over — we can celebrate!
sentences.interview: I have over 5 years of professional experience.
sentences.business: We serve over 50,000 clients across India.
sentences.formal: Over the years, the nation has grown stronger.
sentences.informal: I am OVER the moon about this progress!

words.simpleMeaning: in the space or time separating two things

sentences.daily: Between you and me — my English is improving fast!
sentences.office: We need to decide between these two options.
sentences.interview: The difference between good and great is practice.
sentences.business: Choose between customer service and cost — choose both!
sentences.formal: Every citizen is equal between the law.
sentences.informal: Between us — I think you're doing amazing!

words.simpleMeaning: moving from one side to the other; by means of

sentences.daily: I learn English through listening and speaking.
sentences.office: Send the request through the official channel.
sentences.interview: I got this job through hard work.
sentences.business: We connect with clients through multiple channels.
sentences.formal: Real change comes through collective action.
sentences.informal: I got through — and so will you!

words.simpleMeaning: not having or using something

sentences.daily: Don't speak without thinking first.
sentences.office: This project cannot succeed without your support.
sentences.interview: I spoke without any notes — from memory.
sentences.business: We can't grow without customer feedback.
sentences.formal: A society cannot function without justice.
sentences.informal: Without you, none of this would have happened!

words.simpleMeaning: for the reason that

sentences.daily: I learn English because I want a better life.
sentences.office: We succeeded because we never gave up.
sentences.interview: I got the job because of my strong communication.
sentences.business: Because of our quality, clients stay loyal.
sentences.formal: Follow the law because it protects everyone.
sentences.informal: Because I said so — and because I'm right!

words.simpleMeaning: used to introduce a contrast or exception

sentences.daily: English is hard but very rewarding.
sentences.office: I made mistakes but learned from them.
sentences.interview: The interview was tough but I stayed composed.
sentences.business: We are small but mighty.
sentences.formal: It's a challenge but we must face it.
sentences.informal: It's not easy but it's so worth it!

words.simpleMeaning: used to join words or sentences

sentences.daily: I read and write English every single day.
sentences.office: Work hard and stay consistent.
sentences.interview: I have knowledge and experience to offer.
sentences.business: We build and ship products at great speed.
sentences.formal: Every citizen has rights and responsibilities.
sentences.informal: You and me — unstoppable team!

words.simpleMeaning: used to present an alternative

sentences.daily: You can study in the morning or evening.
sentences.office: Call or email — whichever works for you.
sentences.interview: Hard work or talent — which matters more? Both!
sentences.business: Should we go with A or B strategy?
sentences.formal: Justice or injustice — citizens must choose.
sentences.informal: Study now or regret later — your choice!

words.simpleMeaning: used to introduce a condition

sentences.daily: If I practice daily, I will become fluent.
sentences.office: If there are any issues, contact HR.
sentences.interview: If you prepare well, you will clear the interview.
sentences.business: If our clients succeed, we succeed.
sentences.formal: If we don't act, who will?
sentences.informal: If you believe it, you can achieve it — simple!

words.simpleMeaning: at the time that

sentences.daily: When I practice daily, my English improves.
sentences.office: Call me when you are available.
sentences.interview: When I entered, the panel was already present.
sentences.business: When sales drop, we analyze and act.
sentences.formal: When citizens unite, change happens.
sentences.informal: When you're ready — I'll be here waiting!

words.simpleMeaning: therefore; as a result

sentences.daily: I worked hard, so I saw great results.
sentences.office: The client was unhappy, so we refunded immediately.
sentences.interview: I prepared every day, so I aced the interview.
sentences.business: The product failed, so we pivoted and rebuilt.
sentences.formal: The law was broken, so justice must follow.
sentences.informal: I believed, I worked hard — so I won!

words.simpleMeaning: in spite of the fact that

sentences.daily: Although English is hard, I love learning it.
sentences.office: Although it was late, we finished the project.
sentences.interview: Although nervous, I performed well in the interview.
sentences.business: Although small, our team punches above its weight.
sentences.formal: Although challenging, building a fair society is worth it.
sentences.informal: Although it's hard — I'm doing it anyway!

words.simpleMeaning: used before the first of two alternatives

sentences.daily: I can learn from either books or podcasts.
sentences.office: Either call or email — your choice.
sentences.interview: I am either early or on time — never late.
sentences.business: We can go with either strategy — both work.
sentences.formal: Either follow the law or face the consequences.
sentences.informal: Either way — I'm winning!

words.simpleMeaning: used before the first of two alternatives to say that both are untrue

sentences.daily: Neither failure nor rejection can stop me.
sentences.office: We are neither the cheapest nor the most expensive.
sentences.interview: I am neither nervous nor overconfident.
sentences.business: Neither strategy worked — time for plan C.
sentences.formal: Neither poverty nor power excuses injustice.
sentences.informal: Neither rain nor Monday can stop me today!

words.simpleMeaning: during the time that; at the same time as

sentences.daily: I listen to English podcasts while commuting.
sentences.office: While I take notes, you present the idea.
sentences.interview: While waiting, I reviewed my answers again.
sentences.business: While we focus on growth, we never ignore quality.
sentences.formal: While we debate, others act — let's change that.
sentences.informal: I was smiling while struggling — that's strength!

words.simpleMeaning: up to the time that; up to the point when

sentences.daily: I will practice until I am fluent.
sentences.office: Wait until the client gives approval.
sentences.interview: I practiced until every answer was perfect.
sentences.business: We iterate until the product is right.
sentences.formal: We fight until justice is served.
sentences.informal: I won't stop until I'm exactly where I want to be!

words.simpleMeaning: from a time in the past until now; because

sentences.daily: I have been learning English since January.
sentences.office: Since you asked, let me explain.
sentences.interview: Since college, I have always been goal-oriented.
sentences.business: Since Q1, revenue has grown by 22%.
sentences.formal: Since independence, the nation has grown.
sentences.informal: Since I started practicing, I can't stop!

words.simpleMeaning: despite the fact that; however

sentences.daily: I felt nervous, though I didn't show it.
sentences.office: It's tough, though totally worth the effort.
sentences.interview: The question was hard, though I handled it.
sentences.business: The market is tough, though our strategy is solid.
sentences.formal: It's complex, though we owe it to citizens.
sentences.informal: Though the road is tough — the destination is great!

words.simpleMeaning: up to now; however; still

sentences.daily: I haven't mastered English yet — but I will!
sentences.office: Have you sent the report yet?
sentences.interview: I haven't made it yet — but I am on my way.
sentences.business: We haven't reached the target yet — but we're close.
sentences.formal: Justice hasn't arrived yet — but it will.
sentences.informal: Not there yet — but WATCH me get there!

words.simpleMeaning: the top part of your body with your brain, eyes, and ears

sentences.daily: Use your head — think before you speak.
sentences.office: The head of our department is very supportive.
sentences.interview: Hold your head high in every interview.
sentences.business: He is the head of the company.
sentences.formal: A healthy nation starts with a healthy head (leader).
sentences.informal: Keep your head up — you've got this!

words.simpleMeaning: the body part at the end of your arm

sentences.daily: Give me a hand — I need help.
sentences.office: Hands-on experience is priceless.
sentences.interview: Shake hands firmly — it shows confidence.
sentences.business: Raise your hand if you have a question.
sentences.formal: Give the poor a hand — they need it.
sentences.informal: Give me five! You crushed it!

words.simpleMeaning: the organ used for seeing

sentences.daily: Keep your eye on the goal always.
sentences.office: I keep an eye on all deadlines.
sentences.interview: Maintain eye contact in interviews — it builds trust.
sentences.business: An eye for detail separates great from average.
sentences.formal: An eye for an eye makes the whole world blind.
sentences.informal: I have an eye for good design — it's a gift!

words.simpleMeaning: the organ used for hearing

sentences.daily: Lend me your ear — I have something important to say.
sentences.office: Listen with both ears — open your mind.
sentences.interview: I have a good ear for language and accents.
sentences.business: Keep your ear to the ground — industry trends matter.
sentences.formal: Have an ear for the voice of the people.
sentences.informal: She has an ear for languages — incredible!

words.simpleMeaning: the opening in your face for eating and speaking

sentences.daily: Open your mouth and speak — don't be shy.
sentences.office: Word of mouth is the best marketing.
sentences.interview: I kept my mouth shut and listened carefully.
sentences.business: Our business grew through word-of-mouth referrals.

sentences.formal: Every citizen's skills deserve recognition — start with a resume.
sentences.informal: Resume: 1 page. Impact: unlimited!

words.simpleMeaning: relating to a job requiring special training

sentences.daily: Professional English sets me apart.
sentences.office: Always maintain a professional attitude.
sentences.interview: I presented myself professionally in the interview.
sentences.business: Professional excellence is our company standard.
sentences.formal: Professional governance builds citizen trust.
sentences.informal: Professional and growing — that's me every day!

words.simpleMeaning: following official rules or customs

sentences.daily: I practice formal English for interviews and office.
sentences.office: Use formal language in all client emails.
sentences.interview: The interview was formal — I was prepared.
sentences.business: Formal contracts protect business relationships.
sentences.formal: Formal processes ensure fair governance.
sentences.informal: Formal English: my tool for every professional door!

words.simpleMeaning: not following official rules; casual

sentences.daily: Use informal English with close friends and family.
sentences.office: Informal conversations test natural English fluency.
sentences.interview: The informal part of the interview showed my personality.
sentences.business: Informal team check-ins build stronger bonds.
sentences.formal: Informal civic conversation is also democracy.
sentences.informal: Formal at work. Informal with friends. Perfect balance!

words.simpleMeaning: to build relationships with other professionals

sentences.daily: Network with professionals who are where you want to be.
sentences.office: Network online on LinkedIn — in English!
sentences.interview: I networked effectively at the industry conference.
sentences.business: Network before you need something — it's too late after.
sentences.formal: Networked communities are stronger communities.
sentences.informal: I network daily — and my circle grows every day!

words.simpleMeaning: to work jointly with others

sentences.daily: I collaborate with study partners to improve.
sentences.office: We collaborate across teams to serve clients better.
sentences.interview: I showed I can collaborate and also lead independently.
sentences.business: Collaboration creates products that individuals cannot.
sentences.formal: Citizens collaborating = communities thriving.
sentences.informal: I collaborate. I learn. I win — together!

words.simpleMeaning: to discuss something to reach an agreement

sentences.daily: I will negotiate my salary with confidence.
sentences.office: Negotiating in English is a powerful professional skill.
sentences.interview: I negotiated the offer terms successfully.
sentences.business: Negotiating a better deal requires preparation and clarity.
sentences.formal: Leaders must negotiate for the benefit of all citizens.
sentences.informal: Negotiation skill + English = unbeatable professional!

words.simpleMeaning: the right or ability to enter or use something

sentences.daily: I access English learning resources every day.
sentences.office: Access the portal with your employee ID.
sentences.interview: I accessed 500+ resources for interview prep.
sentences.business: Give customers easy access to support.
sentences.formal: Equal access to justice is a fundamental right.
sentences.informal: I have access to the world — via English!

words.simpleMeaning: to show or introduce; at this time

sentences.daily: I will present my ideas clearly in English.
sentences.office: Please present your findings in tomorrow's meeting.
sentences.interview: I presented for 5 minutes in the final interview round.
sentences.business: Present your solution with confidence and data.
sentences.formal: Leaders must present plans clearly to citizens.
sentences.informal: I present. I impress. I WIN!

words.simpleMeaning: to speak to; a specific location; to deal with

sentences.daily: Please address your concerns directly to me.
sentences.office: Our office address is in the email.
sentences.interview: I addressed every question with clarity.
sentences.business: Address customer grievances within 24 hours.
sentences.formal: Leaders must address citizen concerns — every time.
sentences.informal: Address the elephant in the room — I always do!

words.simpleMeaning: to publicly declare or make known

sentences.daily: I am pleased to announce: I passed my English test!
sentences.office: We are excited to announce our new product launch.
sentences.interview: The manager announced the promotion publicly.
sentences.business: The company announced record-breaking Q4 results.
sentences.formal: Announce policies transparently — citizens deserve it.
sentences.informal: I am excited to announce: Day 1 = COMPLETE!

words.simpleMeaning: to make a formal request; to put into effect

sentences.daily: Apply for the English course today — don't wait.
sentences.office: Please apply for the internal transfer by Friday.
sentences.interview: I applied to 30 companies — 3 callbacks.
sentences.business: Apply the Pareto principle — 20% effort, 80% results.
sentences.formal: Apply the law equally — without exception.
sentences.informal: I applied and I GOT IT — hard work pays!

words.simpleMeaning: to put things in order or plan something

sentences.daily: I arrange my study materials every Sunday.
sentences.office: Please arrange the conference room by 9 AM.
sentences.interview: I arranged all my documents for the interview.
sentences.business: We arrange customer visits to our production facility.
sentences.formal: Arrange elections fairly and transparently.
sentences.informal: Arranged. Organized. Ready to win!

words.simpleMeaning: to state that something is definitely true

sentences.daily: Please confirm your attendance by Friday.
sentences.office: Confirm the meeting details with the client.
sentences.interview: I confirmed the interview slot 24 hours in advance.
sentences.business: Confirm orders before processing payments.
sentences.formal: Confirm election results transparently — citizens need certainty.
sentences.informal: Confirmed! I'm in — and I'm ready!

words.simpleMeaning: to think carefully about something

sentences.daily: Consider all options before making a decision.
sentences.office: Please consider my application for this role.
sentences.interview: I considered every possible interview question.
sentences.business: Consider long-term impact before short-term gains.
sentences.formal: Consider every citizen's voice in policymaking.
sentences.informal: Consider this: I am getting better every single day!

words.simpleMeaning: to give or add to something

sentences.daily: I contribute to my team with English skills and ideas.
sentences.office: Everyone must contribute to the team's success.
sentences.interview: I want to contribute meaningfully to your organization.
sentences.business: We contribute 5% of profits to community projects.
sentences.formal: Every citizen must contribute to national growth.
sentences.informal: I contribute. I collaborate. I create impact!

words.simpleMeaning: to suggest something as good or suitable

sentences.daily: I recommend practicing English for at least 1 hour daily.
sentences.office: I can recommend you for the position — your work is excellent.
sentences.interview: My referral recommended me for the job.
sentences.business: We recommend this product 100% — no hesitation.
sentences.formal: Citizens recommend good leaders — through their votes.
sentences.informal: I recommend this English course to everyone!

words.simpleMeaning: to say or act in reply to something

sentences.daily: Respond to every question clearly and confidently.
sentences.office: Please respond to the client's email by noon.
sentences.interview: I responded calmly — even to tough questions.
sentences.business: Respond to customer feedback within 24 hours.
sentences.formal: Leaders must respond to citizen needs — not ignore them.
sentences.informal: I respond. I adapt. I deliver!

words.simpleMeaning: to put forward an idea for someone to consider

sentences.daily: I suggest practicing every day for best results.
sentences.office: Can I suggest an alternative approach?
sentences.interview: I suggested an improvement during the case study.
sentences.business: We suggest solutions — not just identify problems.
sentences.formal: Suggest policy changes through proper civic channels.
sentences.informal: I suggest: never stop learning. Ever!

words.simpleMeaning: to hand something in for review

sentences.daily: Submit your daily English exercise every morning.
sentences.office: Submit the report before the Friday deadline.
sentences.interview: I submitted my application 3 days early.
sentences.business: Submit invoices within 30 days of delivery.
sentences.formal: Submit your vote — it is your most powerful action.
sentences.informal: Submitted! That felt GREAT!

words.simpleMeaning: to follow the progress or movement of something

sentences.daily: I track my vocabulary growth in a notebook.
sentences.office: Track the project progress in the shared dashboard.
sentences.interview: I tracked every interview question I answered.
sentences.business: We track customer satisfaction weekly.
sentences.formal: Track how public money is spent — citizen right.
sentences.informal: Tracking my progress = knowing I'm on the right path!

words.simpleMeaning: to find the exact size or quantity of something

sentences.daily: I measure my English progress every week.
sentences.office: Measure the results — not just the effort.
sentences.interview: I measured my readiness against 200 practice questions.
sentences.business: We measure success by client outcomes — not our own metrics.
sentences.formal: Measure governance by citizen happiness — not GDP alone.
sentences.informal: Measure. Improve. Repeat. The formula for success!

words.simpleMeaning: to arrange something in a structured way

sentences.daily: I organize my study schedule every Sunday.
sentences.office: Please organize the documents alphabetically.
sentences.interview: I organized my answers using the STAR method.
sentences.business: Organize your team with clear roles and responsibilities.
sentences.formal: Organize civic drives — cleanups, awareness, voting campaigns.
sentences.informal: Organized mind, clear goals, unstoppable results!

words.simpleMeaning: to recognize or name something exactly

sentences.daily: I identify my weaknesses and work on them.
sentences.office: Identify the root cause before solving a problem.
sentences.interview: I identified the exact area where I needed improvement.
sentences.business: We identify customer pain points early.
sentences.formal: Identify inequality and address it — that's leadership.
sentences.informal: I identify challenges. I solve them. Always!

words.simpleMeaning: to show evidence of or prove

sentences.daily: Demonstrate your English skills through daily use.
sentences.office: Can you demonstrate how you handled that challenge?
sentences.interview: I demonstrated leadership ability in every interview round.
sentences.business: Demonstrate your product's value — don't just describe it.
sentences.formal: Leaders must demonstrate integrity — not just promise it.
sentences.informal: I demonstrate growth every week — proof is in the progress!

words.simpleMeaning: to judge or calculate the value of something

sentences.daily: Evaluate your English progress at the end of each week.
sentences.office: We evaluate employee performance quarterly.
sentences.interview: The panel evaluated my technical and soft skills.
sentences.business: Evaluate vendors on quality AND price.
sentences.formal: Citizens must evaluate their leaders honestly.
sentences.informal: I evaluate. I improve. I progress!

words.simpleMeaning: to put a plan or decision into action

sentences.daily: I implement my learning plan every single day.
sentences.office: Let's implement the strategy from Day 1.
sentences.interview: I implemented every interview tip I had learned.
sentences.business: Implement feedback quickly — it shows agility.
sentences.formal: Implement laws fairly — every citizen benefits.
sentences.informal: Plan. Decide. IMPLEMENT. That's my style!

words.simpleMeaning: to make something easier to understand or do

sentences.daily: Simplify complex grammar into daily use.
sentences.office: Simplify the process — remove unnecessary steps.
sentences.interview: I simplified technical answers for the HR panel.
sentences.business: Simplify your product — complexity kills adoption.
sentences.formal: Simplify laws — citizens must be able to understand them.
sentences.informal: I simplify the complex — that's my communication superpower!

words.simpleMeaning: to make something clearer and easier to understand

sentences.daily: Please clarify — I didn't understand the question.
sentences.office: Clarify all doubts before starting the project.
sentences.interview: I clarified every grammar point that confused me.
sentences.business: Clarify expectations with clients at project start.
sentences.formal: Leaders must clarify — not cloud — public understanding.
sentences.informal: Can you clarify? I want to understand fully!

words.simpleMeaning: to rank tasks or goals in order of importance

sentences.daily: I prioritize speaking practice above everything else.
sentences.office: Prioritize client-impacting issues first.
sentences.interview: I prioritized vocabulary and grammar for the interview.
sentences.business: Prioritize projects with the highest ROI.
sentences.formal: Governments must prioritize citizen welfare above all.
sentences.informal: Priorities set. Focus locked. Let's work!

words.simpleMeaning: to make the best use of something

sentences.daily: Optimize your study hours for maximum retention.
sentences.office: Optimize your LinkedIn profile with strong English.
sentences.interview: I optimized my answers using STAR method.
sentences.business: Optimize your product for mobile — India is mobile-first.
sentences.formal: Optimize public services for speed and fairness.
sentences.informal: Optimizing my English every single day!

words.simpleMeaning: to examine something carefully

sentences.daily: I analyze my mistakes to grow faster.
sentences.office: Analyze the data before presenting conclusions.
sentences.interview: I analyzed the company deeply before the interview.
sentences.business: Analyze market trends before making big bets.
sentences.formal: Analyze policies for citizen impact — not just revenue.
sentences.informal: I analyze. I learn. I improve. Unstoppable!

words.simpleMeaning: to examine again or look back at something

sentences.daily: I review my notes every Sunday to reinforce learning.
sentences.office: Please review the contract before signing.
sentences.interview: I reviewed 200 practice questions before the interview.
sentences.business: Review performance quarterly — reward and redirect.
sentences.formal: Citizens must review and scrutinize governance.
sentences.informal: Review complete. Improvements locked in. Moving forward!

words.simpleMeaning: to write down or store information

sentences.daily: Record your practice sessions and listen back.
sentences.office: Record the meeting minutes and share with all.
sentences.interview: I recorded myself speaking and analyzed weaknesses.
sentences.business: Record every transaction — transparency matters.
sentences.formal: Record public decisions — accountability requires it.
sentences.informal: Recording my voice = fastest improvement tool ever!

words.simpleMeaning: to watch something carefully over time

sentences.daily: I monitor my vocabulary growth every week.
sentences.office: Monitor the project status daily.
sentences.interview: I monitored my own interview answers using recordings.
sentences.business: Monitor KPIs weekly — not just at end of quarter.
sentences.formal: Monitor public spending — it's every citizen's right.
sentences.informal: Monitoring. Improving. Never stopping!

words.simpleMeaning: to organize different activities so they work together

sentences.daily: I coordinate my study plan with my daily schedule.
sentences.office: Please coordinate with the client for the delivery date.
sentences.interview: I showed I can coordinate multiple workstreams.
sentences.business: Coordinate across departments for seamless execution.
sentences.formal: Coordinate civic bodies for effective governance.
sentences.informal: Coordinated. Aligned. Executing!

words.simpleMeaning: to carry out or put a plan into action

sentences.daily: Ideas mean nothing without the ability to execute.
sentences.office: I execute my daily study plan without fail.
sentences.interview: I executed my interview preparation flawlessly.
sentences.business: Execute the strategy — don't just present it.
sentences.formal: Execute policies for the good of every citizen.
sentences.informal: Execution is everything — and I excel at it!

words.simpleMeaning: to give a task or responsibility to someone else

sentences.daily: I delegate repetitive tasks to focus on learning.
sentences.office: A good leader knows when to delegate.
sentences.interview: I showed the ability to delegate in leadership interviews.
sentences.business: Delegate to your team — trust and empower them.
sentences.formal: Elected leaders delegate — but not their accountability.
sentences.informal: Delegate wisely. Empower always!

words.simpleMeaning: to officially agree to something

sentences.daily: I approve of this learning method — it works!
sentences.office: Please approve the budget before end of day.
sentences.interview: My application was approved in 48 hours.
sentences.business: The board approved the new market expansion strategy.
sentences.formal: Approve laws that serve the people — not just the powerful.
sentences.informal: APPROVED — and I celebrate every small win!

words.simpleMeaning: to refuse to accept something

sentences.daily: Rejection is redirection — I don't fear it.
sentences.office: We had to reject the proposal — it didn't fit our values.
sentences.interview: I was rejected but learned more from it than from any success.
sentences.business: Reject bad deals — don't compromise on quality.
sentences.formal: Reject injustice — peacefully and powerfully.
sentences.informal: Rejected? Reset. Reapply. Rebuilt. RISE!

words.simpleMeaning: to start or introduce something new

sentences.daily: I am launching my English fluency journey today!
sentences.office: We are launching our new product next Monday.
sentences.interview: I launched into the interview with a strong opening.
sentences.business: Launch with a beta — improve with user feedback.
sentences.formal: Launch civic education programs in every school.
sentences.informal: LAUNCHED! Day 1 is officially underway!

words.simpleMeaning: to change completely and dramatically

sentences.daily: This course will transform my English.
sentences.office: We transformed our product based on user feedback.
sentences.interview: I transformed from a nervous speaker to a confident one.
sentences.business: Technology transformed how we do business.
sentences.formal: Education can transform society — invest in it.
sentences.informal: I AM being transformed. Daily. Powerfully!

words.simpleMeaning: to fill someone with enthusiasm or creative ideas

sentences.daily: I want to inspire others with my English journey.
sentences.office: Our leader inspires by example — not just words.
sentences.interview: The interviewer's career story inspired me greatly.
sentences.business: Inspired teams outperform commanded ones every time.
sentences.formal: Inspire citizens — give them a vision for the future.
sentences.informal: I am inspired — and that energy is CONTAGIOUS!

words.simpleMeaning: an experienced person who advises and guides

sentences.daily: Find a mentor who speaks great English.
sentences.office: I am grateful for my English mentor.
sentences.interview: My mentor helped me prepare for every interview stage.
sentences.business: Every employee deserves a mentor.
sentences.formal: Society needs mentors who guide the next generation.
sentences.informal: My mentor believed in me before I believed in myself!

words.simpleMeaning: the worth of something; to regard highly

sentences.daily: The value of English fluency is immeasurable.
sentences.office: Our core company value is integrity.
sentences.interview: I articulated my value proposition clearly.
sentences.business: Add value before you extract it — always.
sentences.formal: Value the dignity of every citizen.
sentences.informal: Know your value — and never settle for less!

words.simpleMeaning: an increase or development over time

sentences.daily: English fluency is my biggest area of growth.
sentences.office: We hit 35% growth in Q3 — a company record.
sentences.interview: Personal growth is more important than salary.
sentences.business: Growth is the mission — everything else supports it.
sentences.formal: National growth means growth for every citizen.
sentences.informal: GROWTH: the only direction I know!

words.simpleMeaning: abilities that may be developed in the future

sentences.daily: I am unlocking my full potential through English.
sentences.office: This candidate has great potential — hire her!
sentences.interview: I showed my potential from the very first interview round.
sentences.business: Unlock the potential of your most underused employee.
sentences.formal: Unlock the potential of every Indian citizen.
sentences.informal: My potential is UNLIMITED — and I'm just getting started!

words.simpleMeaning: to successfully reach a goal

sentences.daily: I want to achieve my fitness goal this month.
sentences.office: We achieved all our quarterly targets.
sentences.interview: I achieved the highest sales in my previous role.
sentences.business: The company achieved 30% growth in one year.
sentences.formal: The team achieved exceptional results within budget.
sentences.informal: Yes! We finally achieved what we wanted!

words.usageNote: Always used with positive outcomes — a goal, dream, or target. Never use for bad results.

words.simpleMeaning: a plan for managing money

sentences.daily: I always plan a monthly budget for expenses.
sentences.office: The marketing budget was increased by 20%.
sentences.interview: I managed a project budget of ₹10 lakhs.
sentences.business: We must review the annual budget before Q4.
sentences.formal: The committee approved the revised budget proposal.
sentences.informal: That trip is out of my budget!

words.usageNote: As a verb: 'I budget carefully.' As a noun: 'What is our budget?' Both forms are common in business.

words.simpleMeaning: to put money into something to earn more

sentences.daily: I invested in a savings scheme last year.
sentences.office: The company invested ₹5 crore in new machinery.
sentences.interview: I have experience investing in stock markets.
sentences.business: We should invest in digital transformation now.
sentences.formal: The firm invested strategically in emerging markets.
sentences.informal: Don't invest money you can't afford to lose!

words.usageNote: Invest in (a place/thing): 'invest in stocks.' Invest with (a person): 'invest with a broker.'

words.simpleMeaning: money earned after paying all costs

sentences.daily: The small bakery made a good profit last month.
sentences.office: Our Q3 profit exceeded last year by 40%.
sentences.interview: The business turned a profit within six months.
sentences.business: Higher sales volume drives greater profit margins.
sentences.formal: The annual profit report was presented to shareholders.
sentences.informal: We made a nice profit selling old furniture!

words.usageNote: Profit is the money LEFT after all costs are paid. 'We made a profit of ₹2 lakh this month.'

words.simpleMeaning: total income earned by a company

sentences.daily: Small shops count every rupee of revenue.
sentences.office: Our annual revenue grew by 25% this quarter.
sentences.interview: The startup reported ₹1 crore in monthly revenue.
sentences.business: Revenue from digital sales now exceeds offline sales.
sentences.formal: Total revenue for the fiscal year stood at ₹50 crore.
sentences.informal: Their YouTube channel earns good revenue!

words.usageNote: Revenue = all money earned. Profit = revenue minus costs. Both are crucial business metrics.

words.simpleMeaning: to discuss to reach an agreement

sentences.daily: I negotiated a better rent with my landlord.
sentences.office: We negotiated a three-year contract with the supplier.
sentences.interview: Can you negotiate your salary? Always try politely.
sentences.business: The two companies negotiated for weeks before signing.
sentences.formal: Representatives negotiated terms acceptable to all parties.
sentences.informal: I negotiated the price down by ₹5,000!

words.usageNote: Negotiate WITH someone; negotiate FOR something: 'We negotiated with the vendor for a discount.'

words.simpleMeaning: a formal written legal agreement

sentences.daily: Sign a contract before starting any freelance work.
sentences.office: The employment contract was signed by both parties.
sentences.interview: Always read the contract carefully before signing.
sentences.business: We offer a one-year renewable contract for all suppliers.
sentences.formal: The contract stipulates a 30-day notice period.
sentences.informal: Never sign a contract without reading it!

words.usageNote: As a noun: 'Sign the contract.' As a verb: 'The company contracted a new agency.'

words.simpleMeaning: the final date by which something must be done

sentences.daily: The assignment deadline is Friday at midnight.
sentences.office: We must submit the report before the client deadline.
sentences.interview: I never miss a deadline — it's my work ethic.
sentences.business: Missing the deadline may result in penalty charges.
sentences.formal: The project deadline was extended by two weeks.
sentences.informal: I forgot the deadline and had to work all night!

words.usageNote: 'Meet a deadline' (complete on time) and 'miss a deadline' (fail to complete) are key collocations.

words.simpleMeaning: a person with interest or involvement in a project

sentences.daily: Community members are stakeholders in local projects.
sentences.office: All stakeholders attended the quarterly review meeting.
sentences.interview: I presented the project plan to key stakeholders.
sentences.business: Stakeholder feedback was incorporated into the final design.
sentences.formal: Each stakeholder was briefed on their role and responsibilities.
sentences.informal: Even customers are stakeholders — they care about results!

words.usageNote: Stakeholders can be internal (employees) or external (clients, community). Always consider all of them.

words.simpleMeaning: a formal plan or suggestion presented for approval

sentences.daily: I wrote a business proposal for my new startup idea.
sentences.office: The team submitted a detailed project proposal.
sentences.interview: My job application included a research proposal.
sentences.business: The client approved our marketing proposal on Friday.
sentences.formal: The formal proposal outlined objectives, timelines, and costs.
sentences.informal: I proposed a trip to Goa and everyone agreed!

words.usageNote: 'Submit/write/prepare a proposal.' A proposal is a plan you offer for someone else to accept or reject.

words.simpleMeaning: a careful plan to achieve a goal

sentences.daily: My study strategy helped me top the exam.
sentences.office: The company's pricing strategy attracted new customers.
sentences.interview: Tell me about a strategy you used to solve a problem.
sentences.business: A strong digital marketing strategy drove 3× website traffic.
sentences.formal: The board approved a five-year expansion strategy.
sentences.informal: My strategy for traffic? Leave 30 minutes early!

words.usageNote: Strategy is the big-picture PLAN. Tactics are the specific steps to carry out the strategy.

words.simpleMeaning: an important achievement or stage in a project

sentences.daily: Finishing school was a big milestone in my life.
sentences.office: The team celebrated every project milestone on time.
sentences.interview: Getting my first job was a major career milestone.
sentences.business: Launching the app was a milestone for the startup.
sentences.formal: Each milestone was reviewed by the project steering committee.
sentences.informal: My first ₹1 lakh savings was a personal milestone!

words.usageNote: Use 'reach/hit/celebrate a milestone.' It marks meaningful progress, not just any step.

words.simpleMeaning: a collection of work samples or investments

sentences.daily: I built a design portfolio to show to employers.
sentences.office: The investment portfolio includes stocks and mutual funds.
sentences.interview: Please send your portfolio before the interview.
sentences.business: A diversified portfolio reduces investment risk significantly.
sentences.formal: The portfolio was presented to the board of directors.
sentences.informal: My Instagram is basically my photography portfolio!

words.usageNote: For jobs: show your portfolio. For finance: 'manage a portfolio of investments.'

words.simpleMeaning: to officially start or introduce something new

sentences.daily: We launched a new product line last week.
sentences.office: The startup launched its app with a free trial offer.
sentences.interview: I launched my freelance career during the pandemic.
sentences.business: The brand launched a nationwide advertising campaign.
sentences.formal: The company officially launched operations in Southeast Asia.
sentences.informal: They launched the party and everyone showed up!

words.usageNote: 'Launch' suggests something BIG starting officially — a product, campaign, career, or project.

words.simpleMeaning: to grow or expand a business to a larger size

sentences.daily: Small businesses dream of scaling their operations.
sentences.office: We plan to scale our team from 10 to 50 people.
sentences.interview: How did you scale the company to ₹10 crore revenue?
sentences.business: Scaling requires systems, processes, and strong leadership.
sentences.formal: The startup scaled rapidly after receiving Series A funding.
sentences.informal: They scaled the chai stall to 50 outlets nationwide!

words.usageNote: 'Scale' in business = grow systematically. Opposite: 'downsize' or 'scale back.'

words.simpleMeaning: to buy or obtain something, especially a company

sentences.daily: I acquired new skills by taking online courses.
sentences.office: The tech giant acquired a small AI startup.
sentences.interview: During my tenure, I acquired expertise in data analysis.
sentences.business: The company acquired three competitors in two years.
sentences.formal: The corporation acquired a significant market share.
sentences.informal: She acquired a taste for coffee in college!

words.usageNote: Acquire (companies/assets) = buy. Acquire (skills/habits) = develop through effort or time.

words.simpleMeaning: a share of company profits paid to shareholders

sentences.daily: Hard work always pays a dividend in the long run.
sentences.office: Shareholders received a quarterly dividend of ₹5 per share.
sentences.interview: The company declared its highest dividend in five years.
sentences.business: Regular dividend payments attract long-term investors.
sentences.formal: The board voted to increase the annual dividend by 10%.
sentences.informal: Good habits pay dividends — health, wealth, and peace!

words.usageNote: Financial: money paid to investors. General use: 'Hard work pays dividends' = gives rewards later.

words.simpleMeaning: a prediction of what will happen in the future

sentences.daily: The weather forecast says rain tomorrow.
sentences.office: Our sales forecast for Q4 shows 15% growth.
sentences.interview: I can't forecast the exact outcome but I can plan.
sentences.business: The financial forecast was reviewed by all department heads.
sentences.formal: The forecast indicated a strong performance in emerging markets.
sentences.informal: The forecast said sunny — it rained all day!

words.usageNote: As a noun: 'the forecast.' As a verb: 'We forecast strong growth.' Always implies looking ahead.

words.simpleMeaning: a person or company that sells goods or services

sentences.daily: We chose a reliable vendor for office supplies.
sentences.office: The IT vendor provided software maintenance services.
sentences.interview: Can you recommend a vendor for event management?
sentences.business: We shortlisted three vendors and requested proposals.
sentences.formal: The vendor delivered materials two days ahead of schedule.
sentences.informal: The street vendor sold the best momos in town!

words.usageNote: Vendor = anyone who SELLS to you. The company that buys is the client or customer.

words.simpleMeaning: a bill asking for payment for goods or services

sentences.daily: Send me the invoice and I'll process payment today.
sentences.office: The vendor submitted an invoice for ₹25,000.
sentences.interview: As a freelancer, I send invoices every 15 days.
sentences.business: Invoices must include GST number and payment terms.
sentences.formal: The invoice was approved by the accounts department.
sentences.informal: My invoice got lost in their email — classic!

words.usageNote: An invoice is sent BY the seller TO the buyer, requesting payment. A receipt is proof of payment.

words.simpleMeaning: a goal or objective you aim to achieve

sentences.daily: My daily target is to read 20 pages.
sentences.office: We hit our sales target two weeks early!
sentences.interview: I always set clear targets before starting a project.
sentences.business: The marketing team exceeded the acquisition target.
sentences.formal: The annual target was set at ₹1 crore in revenue.
sentences.informal: Target: finish assignment by 9 PM. Status: failed!

words.usageNote: 'Hit/reach/meet a target' (success) vs 'miss a target' (failure). Always be specific when setting targets.

words.simpleMeaning: the difference between cost and selling price

sentences.daily: The profit margin on electronics is usually very thin.
sentences.office: We increased our margin by cutting unnecessary costs.
sentences.interview: What is the margin on this product category?
sentences.business: Higher margins mean more money stays in the business.
sentences.formal: The company maintained healthy margins despite inflation.
sentences.informal: Write in the margin — don't fill the whole page!

words.usageNote: 'Profit margin' = what % of revenue is profit. Wide margins = very profitable. Thin margins = risky.

words.simpleMeaning: a debt or legal obligation

sentences.daily: Loans are a liability until fully repaid.
sentences.office: The company's total liability exceeded ₹5 crore.
sentences.interview: Understand your financial liabilities before investing.
sentences.business: Directors carry personal liability in certain legal cases.
sentences.formal: Assets minus liabilities equals the company's net worth.
sentences.informal: Too many subscriptions become a financial liability!

words.usageNote: In accounting: liability = what you OWE. Opposite of asset = what you OWN. Know both!

words.simpleMeaning: something valuable that a person or company owns

sentences.daily: Your skills are your greatest professional asset.
sentences.office: The company's assets include land, machines, and patents.
sentences.interview: Punctuality is an asset in any workplace.
sentences.business: Brand recognition is a valuable intangible asset.
sentences.formal: The assets were listed in the company's balance sheet.
sentences.informal: A positive attitude is a huge personal asset!

words.usageNote: Assets can be tangible (building, machine) or intangible (brand, goodwill, skills). Both count!

words.simpleMeaning: ownership share in a company

sentences.daily: He got 10% equity in the startup for his work.
sentences.office: Equity investors share in the company's profits and losses.
sentences.interview: I earned sweat equity by working without a salary.
sentences.business: The firm raised ₹2 crore through equity financing.
sentences.formal: Equity was distributed among founding team members.
sentences.informal: She negotiated equity instead of a pay raise!

words.usageNote: Equity = ownership. More equity = more profit share but also more risk. Common in startups.

words.simpleMeaning: to try to win or be better than others

sentences.daily: We compete with global brands every day.
sentences.office: The startup competes in a crowded fintech market.
sentences.interview: I always compete with my past self, not others.
sentences.business: Our company competes on quality, not on price alone.
sentences.formal: The firm competes across 15 markets internationally.
sentences.informal: I only compete in eating contests — and I win!

words.usageNote: Compete WITH someone / FOR something: 'We compete with rivals for the same customers.'

words.simpleMeaning: a standard used to measure or compare something

sentences.daily: This test score is the benchmark for admission.
sentences.office: Our team uses industry benchmarks to set KPIs.
sentences.interview: I benchmark my performance against top performers.
sentences.business: The company benchmarked its processes against global leaders.
sentences.formal: The benchmark report compared 50 companies across 10 metrics.
sentences.informal: My benchmark for a good movie: under 2 hours!

words.usageNote: 'Set a benchmark' = establish a standard. 'Meet/exceed/fall below the benchmark' are common collocations.

words.simpleMeaning: to hire an outside company to do work for you

sentences.daily: Many companies outsource their IT support.
sentences.office: We outsource content writing to freelancers in India.
sentences.interview: Our HR is fully outsourced to a third-party agency.
sentences.business: Outsourcing reduces costs but requires strong vendor management.
sentences.formal: The company outsourced payroll processing for efficiency.
sentences.informal: I outsource cooking on Sundays — Swiggy it is!

words.usageNote: Outsource = give your work to an outside company. In-source = do it yourself internally.

words.simpleMeaning: regular business costs not related to making a product

sentences.daily: Rent and electricity are monthly overhead costs.
sentences.office: Reducing overhead helped the startup become profitable faster.
sentences.interview: High overhead eats into your profit margin quickly.
sentences.business: The company cut overhead by moving to remote work.
sentences.formal: Overhead expenses were reduced by 30% through digitisation.
sentences.informal: My biggest personal overhead? My data plan!

words.usageNote: Overhead = costs you pay regardless of how much you sell — rent, salaries, utilities.

words.simpleMeaning: relating to government or company finances

sentences.daily: The government announced the fiscal budget in February.
sentences.office: Q4 is the last quarter of our fiscal year.
sentences.interview: Fiscal responsibility is key to a healthy economy.
sentences.business: The company's fiscal performance improved year on year.
sentences.formal: The fiscal deficit was reduced through prudent spending cuts.
sentences.informal: My fiscal policy at home: don't spend what you don't have!

words.usageNote: 'Fiscal year' is a company's 12-month financial period, often April–March in India.

words.simpleMeaning: total sales made, or rate at which employees leave

sentences.daily: The company's annual turnover crossed ₹100 crore.
sentences.office: High employee turnover is costly for any organisation.
sentences.interview: Our retail turnover increased by 18% this holiday season.
sentences.business: Staff turnover in the industry averages 25% annually.
sentences.formal: The turnover figure was reported in the annual accounts.
sentences.informal: High staff turnover? Maybe improve the free snacks!

words.usageNote: 'Business turnover' = total sales. 'Staff turnover' = rate of employees leaving. Two very different meanings!

words.simpleMeaning: to change an industry dramatically with a new idea

sentences.daily: Smartphones disrupted the camera and map industries.
sentences.office: Our startup is set to disrupt the edtech market.
sentences.interview: Don't be afraid to disrupt your own business model.
sentences.business: Digital payment apps disrupted traditional banking methods.
sentences.formal: The new entrant disrupted established players with aggressive pricing.
sentences.informal: Jio disrupted India's telecom industry completely!

words.usageNote: In business: disruption = positive change that upends traditional methods. Disruptors create new market leaders.

words.simpleMeaning: something that motivates a person to act

sentences.daily: Performance bonuses are a great work incentive.
sentences.office: The government offers tax incentives for electric vehicles.
sentences.interview: Give employees clear incentives to perform better.
sentences.business: A referral incentive program doubled customer acquisition.
sentences.formal: Financial incentives were offered to early adopters.
sentences.informal: The incentive? Free pizza if we finish by 6 PM!

words.usageNote: 'Create/offer/provide incentives.' Incentive = something desirable that pushes people to take action.

words.simpleMeaning: to successfully enter a new market

sentences.daily: The brand penetrated Tier 2 cities with small packs.
sentences.office: Our goal is to penetrate the Southeast Asian market.
sentences.interview: How did you penetrate a market with 10 competitors?
sentences.business: Low pricing helped penetrate the rural healthcare market.
sentences.formal: The firm penetrated the premium segment through partnerships.
sentences.informal: We penetrated the local food market with fusion dishes!

words.usageNote: 'Market penetration' = gaining customers in a new or competitive market. A key growth strategy.

words.simpleMeaning: the estimated market value of a company

sentences.daily: The startup's valuation reached $1 billion — a unicorn!
sentences.office: Investors base funding decisions on company valuation.
sentences.interview: Getting a realistic valuation is crucial before fundraising.
sentences.business: The valuation rose 10× after the product launch.
sentences.formal: An independent valuation was commissioned before the acquisition.
sentences.informal: My self-valuation: priceless — but I'm open to offers!

words.usageNote: 'Pre-money valuation' (before investment) vs 'post-money valuation' (after). Important startup terms.

words.simpleMeaning: a person who starts and runs their own business

sentences.daily: Every entrepreneur starts with an idea and courage.
sentences.office: She became an entrepreneur at 23 with just ₹50,000.
sentences.interview: Tell me what motivates you as an entrepreneur.
sentences.business: Successful entrepreneurs fail many times before they succeed.
sentences.formal: The entrepreneur raised ₹2 crore in seed funding.
sentences.informal: I'm an entrepreneur — I sell samosas and I'm the boss!

words.usageNote: Entrepreneur = someone who takes financial risk to start a business. Plural: entrepreneurs. Adj: entrepreneurial.

words.simpleMeaning: a person with interest in or affected by a business

sentences.daily: Customers are the most important stakeholders.
sentences.office: All stakeholders attended the quarterly review meeting.
sentences.interview: I presented the business plan to key stakeholders.
sentences.business: Stakeholder buy-in is critical before any major change.
sentences.formal: Each stakeholder was briefed on their roles.
sentences.informal: Even my mom is a stakeholder in my exam results!

words.usageNote: Internal stakeholders: employees, management. External: customers, government, investors. Both matter.

words.simpleMeaning: a company's identity, name, and reputation

sentences.daily: Apple is the most valuable brand in the world.
sentences.office: We need to build a strong brand image online.
sentences.interview: How would you improve a brand's social media presence?
sentences.business: Consistent messaging is the foundation of brand identity.
sentences.formal: The brand was repositioned to target younger consumers.
sentences.informal: My brand: reliable, funny, and usually late by 10 minutes!

words.usageNote: 'Brand' is not just a logo — it's the entire experience and perception customers have of a company.

words.simpleMeaning: when two companies combine to form one

sentences.daily: The merger created the country's largest bank.
sentences.office: Post-merger integration is one of the hardest processes.
sentences.interview: The merger was approved by the board unanimously.
sentences.business: Both companies benefited from the cost savings post-merger.
sentences.formal: The proposed merger underwent regulatory review for six months.
sentences.informal: It was less a merger, more a hostile takeover!

words.usageNote: 'Merger' = two companies agree to join. 'Acquisition' = one company buys another. Both reshape industries.

words.simpleMeaning: profit share paid to shareholders

sentences.daily: Hard work always pays a dividend eventually.
sentences.office: The company declared a dividend of ₹5 per share.
sentences.interview: Investors choose dividend stocks for steady income.
sentences.business: The dividend yield was 3.5% — attractive for investors.
sentences.formal: Dividends were paid quarterly to all registered shareholders.
sentences.informal: Good habits pay dividends for life — try it!

words.usageNote: Financial dividend = money paid to shareholders. Broader use: 'the dividend of hard work = success.'

words.simpleMeaning: an asset pledged to secure a loan

sentences.daily: He used his house as collateral for the business loan.
sentences.office: No collateral is needed for small personal loans below ₹2 lakh.
sentences.interview: Banks require collateral for large credit facilities.
sentences.business: The startup offered shares as collateral for the bridge loan.
sentences.formal: Collateral damage can extend beyond the intended target.
sentences.informal: I offered my scooter as collateral — bank said no!

words.usageNote: 'Put up/offer collateral.' In banking: collateral protects the lender if borrower can't repay.

words.simpleMeaning: a period of economic decline

sentences.daily: The 2008 recession affected millions of jobs worldwide.
sentences.office: Companies must plan for a potential economic recession.
sentences.interview: During the recession, many small businesses shut down.
sentences.business: Cost-cutting is essential when a recession hits.
sentences.formal: The recession was caused by a collapse in the housing market.
sentences.informal: Even a chocolate recession would be too much to bear!

words.usageNote: Two consecutive quarters of negative GDP growth = recession. Protect savings and reduce debt when it hits.

words.simpleMeaning: the rise in prices of goods over time

sentences.daily: Inflation has made groceries very expensive this year.
sentences.office: The RBI raised interest rates to control inflation.
sentences.interview: Inflation affects purchasing power and savings value.
sentences.business: High inflation erodes the real value of your income.
sentences.formal: Inflation remained above the target rate for three consecutive months.
sentences.informal: Inflation is real — my chai went from ₹5 to ₹20!

words.usageNote: Inflation = prices going UP. Deflation = prices going DOWN. India targets 4% inflation (RBI policy).

words.simpleMeaning: money given by the government to reduce costs

sentences.daily: The government provides a subsidy on LPG cylinders.
sentences.office: Farmers receive a fertilizer subsidy to reduce costs.
sentences.interview: The EV industry benefits from government subsidies.
sentences.business: Remove the subsidy and prices will rise immediately.
sentences.formal: Agricultural subsidies were reviewed as part of fiscal reform.
sentences.informal: Without that canteen subsidy, lunch would cost ₹200!

words.usageNote: Subsidy = financial support given to REDUCE prices or help certain industries or groups.

words.simpleMeaning: the reduction in value of an asset over time

sentences.daily: Depreciation reduces the book value of machinery each year.
sentences.office: The car's depreciation was 20% in its first year.
sentences.interview: Accounting rules define how to calculate asset depreciation.
sentences.business: High depreciation means lower taxable income for the business.
sentences.formal: Depreciation was calculated using the straight-line method.
sentences.informal: My phone's value depreciated the moment I walked out of the store!

words.usageNote: Depreciation = assets losing value over time. Appreciation = gaining value. Real estate usually appreciates.

words.simpleMeaning: to sell assets to convert them into cash

sentences.daily: He had to liquidate his shares to pay urgent bills.
sentences.office: The company liquidated non-core assets to raise capital.
sentences.interview: During the crisis, they were forced to liquidate inventory.
sentences.business: Liquidating assets quickly may result in lower prices.
sentences.formal: The firm was ordered to liquidate following insolvency proceedings.
sentences.informal: I liquidated my candy bar collection — now just crumbs!

words.usageNote: 'Liquidate' = turn into liquid cash. A company that closes down must liquidate all assets.

words.simpleMeaning: a collection of investments or work samples

sentences.daily: My investment portfolio includes stocks, gold, and funds.
